articles.headline: Big Tech aims to sell cheaper AI globally, can India benefit?
articles.summary: Big Tech is investing billions to reduce AI model training and running costs, gaining a significant advantage. This could make AI more affordable, with token prices already down 75%. India is behind, but state-funded Sarvam and Sequoia-backed Avataar are emerging. This could offer IT firms large deployment deals or threaten process-based jobs.

articles.headline: El Niño: Is the worst ever impact expected this year?
articles.summary: Forecasters fear the current El Niño could be the strongest on record, potentially exceeding 1982-83's 2.5°C rise, possibly reaching 3°C. This phenomenon typically reduces rainfall in Asia, including India, threatening agriculture and potentially stoking inflation. The government has contingency plans in place.

articles.headline: India's manufacturing inflation highest since August 2022
articles.summary: India's wholesale inflation for manufacturing surged 7.5% year-on-year in May 2026, marking its highest level since August 2022. The sharpest cost pressures were observed in the manufacture of chemicals and chemical products (13.4%), and basic metals (12.3%). Other notable rises include electrical equipment (11.3%) and textiles (10.2%).

articles.headline: India’s low female workforce despite declining fertility puzzling
articles.summary: India faces a unique demographic puzzle: declining fertility rates coexist with low female labor force participation, contradicting global trends. This "marriage penalty" sees women leaving work due to disproportionate domestic duties, exacerbated by early marriage and childbirth. Structural barriers push women into precarious informal roles. Policies should focus on institutional support for women's workforce integration.

articles.headline: Banking reforms panel to consider consolidation, FDI cap hike
articles.summary: The finance ministry may ask a new banking reforms committee, possibly headed by M. Nagaraju, to explore merging 12 state-run banks into five. It may also recommend increasing the FDI cap in public sector banks from 20% to 49% to attract more global capital and strengthen balance sheets.

articles.headline: India’s strategic oil reserves only cover 9-10 days
articles.summary: A CEEW report revealed India's strategic petroleum reserves cover only 9-10 days of crude imports, significantly less than import-dependent countries like Japan and South Korea, which maintain over 200 days of reserves.

articles.headline: India's jet fuel exports to Europe return to 2025 levels
articles.summary: India's jet fuel exports to Europe have rebounded to pre-January EU ban levels on Russian-origin fuel. Kpler and LSEG data show around 62,000-64,000 barrels per day (bpd) of jet fuel imported by the EU from India, particularly Italy from Jamnagar refinery.

articles.headline: India’s defence production surges to ₹1.78 trillion in FY26
articles.summary: India's annual defence production reached an all-time high of ₹1.78 trillion in FY26, a 15.6% growth over FY25 and 110% increase since 2020-21. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh highlighted this as a clear indicator of the country’s expanding defence industrial base.

articles.headline: RBI infuses ₹72,300 crore liquidity into banking system
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) infused ₹72,300 crore transient liquidity into the banking system through two Variable Rate Repo (VRR) auctions. This action followed a narrowing of surplus liquidity due to advance tax payments, with auctions at a cut-off rate of 5.26%.

articles.headline: IEA projects oil supply glut in 2027 with US-Iran peace deal
articles.summary: The IEA projects a global crude oil market surplus in 2027 as the US and Iran approach a peace deal, potentially reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Global oil demand is expected to rise moderately by 2 mbpd to 105.3 mbpd, contributing to the oversupply.

articles.headline: Pakistan extends airspace ban on Indian aircraft till 24 July
articles.summary: Pakistan extended its airspace restrictions on Indian-registered aircraft by another month until 24 July. The ban, imposed in April 2025 after the Pahalgam attack, impacts both civil and military Indian aircraft.

articles.headline: US-Iran peace deal may revive India’s IPO market in H2
articles.summary: The proposed US-Iran peace deal is expected to revive India's IPO market in H2 2026, driven by listings like NSE, Manipal Health, Jio Platforms, and Zepto. Investment bankers anticipate stronger market sentiment, with companies raising significant capital through public offerings.

articles.headline: Nvidia pitches affordable AI access for India
articles.summary: Nvidia's chief software architect, Jonathan Ross, advocates for affordable AI access in India, emphasizing reducing "cost per intelligence." He believes this is key for AI's transformative impact, enabling deployment of capable models at prices like ₹99 per chatbot query. He also notes India's infrastructure ambitions.

articles.headline: Ambani aims for Reliance to be AI-native giant
articles.summary: Mukesh Ambani wants Reliance Industries to become an "AI-native" conglomerate, embedding AI across all workflows from recruitment to business planning and revenue forecasting. This strategy, dubbed the 'RIL manifesto', makes AI adoption a key performance metric for senior leadership, reflecting a technology-first approach.

articles.headline: Edible oil prices may fall up to 10%
articles.summary: Retail edible oil prices are expected to ease by up to 10% in coming months, stabilizing through the festival season. This is due to an anticipated US-Iran peace deal reopening the Strait of Hormuz, lowering freight costs, and increased palm oil production from Indonesia and Malaysia from July.

articles.headline: Govt makes food safety permits perpetual, eases business
articles.summary: India has reformed food safety licensing by making permits perpetual, eliminating periodic renewals, to ease compliance for 6.7 million food businesses. The new FSSAI rules also bring over 1 million street vendors into the formal regulatory framework and significantly revise turnover thresholds for licensing.

articles.headline: Embassy Reit leases 830,000 sq ft to Target Corp. India
articles.summary: Embassy Office Parks Reit has leased 830,000 sq ft office space at Embassy Manyata Business Park in Bengaluru to Target Corp. India, a GCC, for ₹1,250 crore over a 10-year tenure. This marks one of the largest GCC deals, signaling massive expansion by Target and strong demand in key hubs like Bengaluru.

articles.headline: Oil drop eases pressure on OMCs, boosts earnings
articles.summary: The imminent US-Iran peace deal and reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, causing Brent crude to drop below $80/barrel, significantly relieves Indian state-run oil marketing companies (OMCs) like IOC, BPCL, and HPCL. This improves their integrated margins (refining + marketing) and could boost earnings, though excise duty rollbacks remain a risk.

articles.headline: FPIs hike Sensex options overnight positions for first time
articles.summary: Foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) created overnight positions or open interest (OI) in BSE’s Sensex options in FY26, a first since their May 2023 relaunch. FPI OI value reached ₹20,056 crore by March-end 2026, from zero in FY25, indicating growing participation, especially in monthly options.

articles.headline: Mid, small-cap surge signals rising risk appetite
articles.summary: Since the US-Iran war began, mid- and small-cap (SMID) stocks have outperformed large-caps, with their share in Nifty 500 market capitalization hitting a record 35.8%. This surge reflects a growing risk appetite among investors seeking stronger earnings growth. However, high valuations and potential economic disruptions pose risks to the rally's endurance.

articles.headline: AIFs urge Sebi to reverse pro-rata rules
articles.summary: Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) are pressing Sebi to reverse its nearly two-year-old pro-rata rules, which mandate identical drawdown terms and equalized distributions for investors. Industry executives argue these rules undermine negotiated fee arrangements, complicate fund operations, and could deter large investors critical for private-market fundraising.

articles.headline: Retail-heavy stocks outperform amid war-led volatility
articles.summary: Stocks with dominant retail non-promoter shareholders have outperformed mutual fund and FPI-dominated firms since the West Asia war, delivering a median 6.4% return. This comes after a steeper correction in Q4FY26 for retail-heavy companies. The rebound reflects retail investor confidence and a recovery in risk appetite, though sustainability depends on earnings and valuations.

articles.headline: Adani Green seeks $1 billion offshore loan
articles.summary: Adani Green Energy Ltd is seeking to raise $1 billion via offshore loans in two tranches over three months, its first overseas borrowing since settling US SEC allegations. The five-year facility, priced over SOFR, will fund capital expenditure and other corporate purposes, removing a key overhang for the group.

articles.headline: Air India SATS targets cargo-led growth with ₹600 cr capex
articles.summary: Air India SATS Airport Services (AISATS) plans to invest ₹600 crore in FY27 and double revenue to ₹3,000-3,500 crore over five years. The 50:50 Tata-SATS JV is expanding beyond ground handling into cargo, warehousing, and airport logistics infrastructure, driven by non-aeronautical revenue streams.

articles.headline: G7 aims to cap China’s rare earth supply at 60%
articles.summary: G7 countries agreed to cap any single country's supply of rare earths and permanent magnets at 60% of their imports by 2030, aiming to reduce reliance on China. They also plan sector-specific quotas for critical minerals and a platform to boost recycling and new mining projects globally.

articles.headline: Trump joins G7 pledge supporting Ukraine, sanctions Russia
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump joined other G7 leaders in a unified pledge of support for Ukraine and fresh sanctions against Russia. This commitment, made at the G7 summit in France, follows Trump's earlier skepticism and aims to bolster Kyiv's leverage in potential peace talks.

articles.headline: UAE aims to end dependency on Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The UAE is pursuing an ambitious plan to eliminate its dependence on the Strait of Hormuz, regardless of its reopening. This involves a major expansion of eastern ports like Dibba, Fujairah, and Khor Fakkan, building new harbors, and significant investments in pipelines, rail, and road networks to reroute energy and commodities.

articles.headline: G7 leaders back Trump’s Iran deal for Strait reopening
articles.summary: G7 leaders supported US President Donald Trump’s tentative agreement with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and extend a ceasefire, despite scant details. The accord, expected Friday, outlines steps for Iran to sell oil without restrictions if a final nuclear deal is reached, aiming to end fighting in Lebanon as well.

articles.headline: Elon Musk uses SpaceX funds to boost AI
articles.summary: Elon Musk is using SpaceX's $86 billion war chest to rapidly advance its AI capabilities, acknowledging past lags behind rivals. SpaceX acquired Musk-controlled xAI, which makes Grok, and coding agent Cursor, to attract enterprise customers. The strategy aims to address AI's high costs and competition for talent, despite xAI's reported losses.

articles.headline: Mumbai reservoirs' water stock at 10.35%
articles.summary: Mumbai's reservoirs hold only 10.35% of their total water stock due to a delayed monsoon. The BMC has suspended water supply to construction sites, indicating a critical situation.

articles.headline: Brookfield, BCI, GIC divest 7.3% stake in Altius
articles.summary: Brookfield, BCI, and GIC raised ₹4,660 crore by divesting a 7.3% stake in Altius Telecom Infrastructure Trust through open market transactions.

articles.headline: Wipro to certify 10,000 on Anthropic’s Claude models
articles.summary: Wipro plans to certify 10,000 front-line delivery experts on Anthropic's Claude AI models over the next 18 months, indicating a focus on AI skill development within its workforce.

articles.headline: JLR pays 2% dividend to Tata Motors in FY26
articles.summary: Jaguar Land Rover paid a 2% dividend to Tata Motors in FY26, despite the British brand recording its first loss in three years.

articles.headline: Freight rates rise 2% since March amid diesel costs
articles.summary: Freight rates have approximately increased by 2% since March, leaving fleet operators needing a hike to offset rising diesel prices, according to Kotak Equities.

articles.headline: Manipal Hospitals targets $1 billion IPO by next month
articles.summary: Manipal Health Enterprises, backed by Temasek, plans to launch a $1 billion IPO as early as next month, targeting a $10 billion valuation. This could be India's first billion-dollar IPO of the year, potentially building market momentum after a slow start.

articles.headline: ACC to expand cement capacity, deepen RMC footprint
articles.summary: ACC Ltd chairman Karan Adani announced plans to expand cement capacity in a calibrated manner, deepen its ready-mix concrete footprint, and accelerate low-carbon technologies adoption. He noted strong long-term Indian economy growth but moderate near-term demand conditions.

articles.headline: BSE-listed firms' market cap reclaims $5 trillion
articles.summary: The market capitalization of BSE-listed companies reached the $5-trillion mark on Wednesday, with investors' wealth rising by ₹22.78 trillion. Equity benchmark indices rallied for the fourth consecutive session, supported by softening crude oil prices following a US-Iran peace deal.

articles.headline: Gold price tumbles ₹4,800, silver down ₹5,300
articles.summary: Gold prices plunged by ₹4,800 to ₹1.54 lakh/10 gm, and silver plummeted by ₹5,300 to ₹2.55 lakh/kg in the national capital on Wednesday. This fall reflects weak domestic demand, a strong rupee, and global trends, as investors shifted to equity markets for higher returns.

articles.headline: Vedanta Power evaluates foray into hydro, battery, nuclear energy
articles.summary: Newly listed Vedanta Power Ltd is exploring diversification into hydro, battery storage, and nuclear energy as part of a long-term plan. The company recognizes nuclear energy as a clean, reliable power source vital for India's energy transition, aiming for expansion via brownfield projects.

articles.headline: Dhruva Space, ICEYE partner for satellite systems, Earth observation
articles.summary: Hyderabad-based Dhruva Space and Finland’s ICEYE signed an MoU to collaborate on developing and manufacturing satellite buses and other space systems. This partnership aims to expand access to Earth observation data and analytics in India, leveraging complementary strengths in space engineering and SAR expertise.

articles.headline: Cognizant's stock plummets despite outperforming rivals
articles.summary: Despite beating rivals in revenue growth (7% in 2025) and securing mega deals, Cognizant's stock has fallen over 38% this year, hitting a six-year low. Investors fear AI-led revenue deflation and question IT services relevance. CEO Ravi Kumar is restructuring, laying off 4,000 employees, and shifting to flatter, AI-integrated models to adapt.

articles.headline: Free portfolio reviews by fintech apps pose risks
articles.summary: Fintech apps offering "free portfolio reviews" often use them as disguised product pitches, recommending financial products that may not align with an investor's goals or risk profile. These automated tools lack financial context, prioritize returns over suitability, and can rely on hindsight or recency bias, leading to uncoordinated and potentially costly portfolio changes.

articles.headline: Assess gold allocation based on long-term returns, not rally
articles.summary: Gold prices surged over the last year, but experts advise evaluating gold allocation based on long-term return profiles rather than recent performance. Equity typically outperforms gold historically. Gold can serve as a defense asset, with a 10-15% portfolio allocation. Investors should avoid large lump-sum investments due to overheated prices and consider staggered ETF investments.

articles.headline: NRIs can open tax-free, repatriable NRE fixed deposits
articles.summary: Non-resident Indians (NRIs) can open NRE fixed deposits in Indian banks using inward remittances, enjoying tax-free interest and full repatriability. These deposits allow NRIs to transfer funds globally without restrictions and can also be used to obtain loans for various purposes in India or abroad.

articles.headline: Decoding circle rates, market prices, and home loan valuation
articles.summary: In India, property circle rates often diverge from market prices, creating challenges for homebuyers during loan valuation. Banks assess properties based on their internal valuation, which may differ significantly from the agreement value or circle rate, potentially leading to a funding gap for buyers. Buyers should get an early valuation read and maintain liquidity buffers.

articles.headline: Art and culture events flourish this summer
articles.summary: Summer art calendars are bustling with diverse exhibitions across India. Showcases include Atul Kasbekar’s photographic exhibition "Honest," exploring actors beyond their personas; "Indigenous Accents" by Raj Kishore Gupta, linking land, memory, and culture; and Afra Eisma's textile works as sites of reflection at MAP, Bengaluru.

articles.headline: JR creates immersive cave art on Paris’s Pont Neuf
articles.summary: French street artist JR has transformed Paris's Pont Neuf, its oldest bridge, into an immersive artificial cavern. The free public installation, open until June 28, uses printed fabric, air, and olfactory effects to create a sensory experience. It includes sound by Thomas Bangalter and an augmented-reality experience via Snap.

articles.headline: Monument Valley eatery reflects Navajo cuisine, history
articles.summary: Goulding’s Stagecoach Dining Room in Monument Valley offers Navajo frybread and cuisine, reflecting the indigenous community's history and renewed voice. Originally built on Navajo land without local ownership, the diner was acquired by the sovereign Navajo Nation in 2023, with revenues now supporting the community and preserving traditions like the "Three Sisters" food system.

articles.headline: India, US 'Very Close' to Inking a Trade Deal, Says Trump
articles.summary: PM Modi and US President Trump met on G7 sidelines. Modi raised seafarers' safety in the Gulf, emphasizing Strait of Hormuz importance. Trump lauded Modi's West Asia peace efforts, stating the two nations are "very close" to a trade deal negotiated for over a year.

articles.headline: Jio Plans Over 1,600 LEO Satellites for Broadband Services
articles.summary: Reliance Jio proposes to launch 1,600-1,650 Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellites at 650 km altitude in 2-3 years for broadband and direct-to-device services. The proposal is with IN-SPACe. This marks an Indian entity's first entry into the LEO segment, requiring $10-15 billion investment, with government support sought for ITU filings.

articles.headline: Shiv Sena UBT Warns Rebel MPs to Resign or Face Sainiks' Ire
articles.summary: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut asked rebel Lok Sabha MPs to quit their seats or face party workers' anger, alleging a `50 crore purchase tag per deserter. The party filed a caveat with the Speaker to prevent recognition of any breakaway group. Nine UBT MPs are under party whip amidst fears of a two-thirds split, possibly shifting NDA's balance of power.

articles.headline: ED Probes `95 Crore Foreign Funds Routed to Maoist Areas
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate will file a money laundering case and ask CBI to register a UAPA case over `95 crore in foreign funds allegedly routed to Maoist-affected areas via US-based Truist Bank debit cards. The funds were withdrawn through 3,200 transactions, with `3.2 crore from a single ATM in Dhamtari. Digital evidence was allegedly deleted after ED searches.

articles.headline: Several Sena UBT MLAs in Touch With Shinde
articles.summary: Maharashtra Deputy CM Eknath Shinde's Shiv Sena plans to poach several of the 20 Shiv Sena (UBT) MLAs. Sources claim it's "almost a done deal" after the Lok Sabha MP defections. Uddhav Thackeray has called meetings for MPs, MLAs, and MLCs, indicating awareness of Shinde's moves. Sena UBT hopes to retain at least two MPs for the parliamentary board meeting.

articles.headline: CJI Vows to Fix Accountability for Misplaced Urgent Files in SC Registry
articles.summary: The Supreme Court, led by CJI Surya Bikash Kant, took serious note of an advocate's complaint that an urgent case filed over 10 days ago hadn't been listed due to allegedly misplaced papers by the registry. CJI Kant expressed strong disapproval, assuring an investigation and accountability for the lapse, directing a formal complaint.

articles.headline: US Restores ‘Pacific Command’ Name; Displays Inaccurate India Map
articles.summary: The US Department of Defence restored the name 'US Pacific Command,' reversing an eight-year-old decision. Concurrently, its website displayed an inaccurate map of India, excluding PoK and Aksai Chin, triggering political criticism. New Delhi maintains J&K and Ladakh are integral parts of India, accessed in 1947.

articles.headline: Karnataka Congress Launches Voter Outreach Ahead of Electoral Roll Revision
articles.summary: Karnataka's ruling Congress will launch a statewide campaign from June 23-30 to raise awareness about the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls. The party fears deletion of traditional support base voters (SC/ST, minorities, backward classes). Leaders like Shivakumar warned residents that failing to update voter details could lead to losing welfare scheme benefits.

articles.headline: Kudankulam Nuclear Plant Unit-5 Gets Reactor Vessel, Reaching Milestone
articles.summary: NPCIL completed the installation of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) at Unit-5 of the Kudankulam Nuclear Power Project (KKNPP) in Tamil Nadu, with Russian support. This 320-tonne RPV houses the nuclear reactor core. Once all six reactors are operational, KKNPP will be India's largest nuclear power facility with a 6,000 MWe capacity.

articles.headline: India, Russia Developing Smaller, Hypersonic BrahMos Missiles
articles.summary: Russian Ambassador Denis Alipov announced India and Russia are jointly developing smaller and hypersonic variants of BrahMos missiles. This will enhance India's multi-domain strike capabilities, building on the system's expansion into land, naval, submarine, and air-launched domains, creating a precision strike ecosystem.

articles.headline: Karnataka CM Shivakumar Seeks Water Share and Bengaluru Infra Boost
articles.summary: Karnataka CM DK Shivakumar's first wish list to the Centre prioritizes securing river water shares and major infrastructure upgrades for Bengaluru. He urged intervention on the Mekedatu project and resolution of the Krishna Water Dispute Tribunal II award. Shivakumar sought a `26,000 crore special grant for Bengaluru's infra projects, including metro and road upgrades.

articles.headline: Energy Costs May Drop if Oil Nears $70/B, Improving Inflation Outlook
articles.summary: Economists predict a decline in crude oil prices towards $70 per barrel, driven by a proposed US-Iran peace deal, could lower energy costs for consumers and moderate imported inflation. This de-escalation in West Asia conflict revives optimism for India's inflation and growth outlook, despite below-normal monsoon forecasts.

articles.headline: Crypto 'OTC Deals' Above $10,000 Under FIU-IND Scrutiny
articles.summary: The Financial Intelligence Unit-India (FIU-IND) is scrutinizing large over-the-counter (OTC) cryptocurrency deals, asking top exchanges to share data for transactions exceeding $10,000. These off-market trades, often by closely-held entities, make beneficial owner identification difficult. Concerns include ease of withdrawing cryptos to private wallets and potential for fake IDs in KYC.

articles.headline: Food Inflation Rising, Monsoon Risks Threaten Further Price Hikes
articles.summary: Food inflation is increasing, with prices of spices, eggs, dairy, tea, and coffee up 6-12%. Economists expect further price pressure due to uneven crop cycles from heatwaves and potentially deficient monsoon rains, coupled with higher input and logistics costs. HDFC Bank estimates Q2 FY27 food & beverage inflation at 5.8%.

articles.headline: EV Manufacturers Seek Financing & Payment Security to Drive Adoption
articles.summary: Electric vehicle manufacturers urged the Centre to introduce enabling financing and robust payment security mechanisms to accelerate electric mobility adoption and boost domestic manufacturing. They also sought faster testing and certification from ARAI and ICAT, and expanded charging infrastructure. Minister Kumaraswamy reiterated India's net-zero goal by 2070.

articles.headline: `12,000 Crore Incentive Scheme Planned for Local Battery Part Manufacturing
articles.summary: India is preparing a `12,000 crore incentive scheme to boost local manufacturing of advanced cell battery components like cathode and anode active materials, electrolysers, and copper foil separators. The aim is to cut import dependence and support emission-free vehicles and defence. This supplements the `18,100 crore PLI scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cells (ACC).

articles.headline: CPPIB to Invest `6,745 Crore in CtrlS Data Centres and JV
articles.summary: Canada Pension Plan Investment Board (CPPIB) will invest up to C$1 billion (`6,745 crore) with CtrlS Datacenters. CPPIB will acquire 8.2% in CtrlS for `4,000 crore and establish a JV to develop hyperscale data centre campuses in Mumbai, Chennai, Hyderabad, Bengaluru, and Pune. The JV aims to build AI-ready infrastructure, with CPPIB holding 48%.

articles.headline: West Asia Crisis Eases, But Consumer Price Cuts Unlikely Soon
articles.summary: Despite crude oil prices slipping below $80 a barrel due to eased West Asia tensions, Indian consumers are unlikely to see immediate price cuts for daily goods. Manufacturers have high raw material inventories purchased at higher rates. FMCG and white goods companies expect stable input costs for months, with price corrections only by Sept-Oct.

articles.headline: Airlines' Operating Profit to Decline This Fiscal Amid Rising Costs
articles.summary: Domestic airlines' operating profit is expected to fall this fiscal. Elevated aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices, airspace restrictions, and rupee depreciation are increasing operating costs. CRISIL Ratings forecasts a 10-15% decline in aggregate operating profit (FY26) and a 12-16% rise in cost per available seat kilometre (CASK), with lease rentals increasing due to fleet expansion.

articles.headline: India Ranks as APAC's Most AI-ready Healthcare Market
articles.summary: A Bain & Company report states India is one of Asia Pacific's most AI-ready healthcare markets, with 78% of consumers using Generative AI (GenAI) to understand diagnoses and treatment options. This highlights the rapid adoption and integration of AI technologies within India's healthcare sector.

articles.headline: Harmony Infra Acquires Horizon Residences for `250 Crore
articles.summary: NCR-based realty developer Harmony Infra acquired complete ownership of The Horizon Residences in Indirapuram, Ghaziabad, for `250 crore. The firm paid `150 crore to the bank and `100 crore to the existing promoter. Harmony Infra plans to invest another `550 crore to complete the 264-residence ultra-luxury project, expecting `1,600 crore revenue.

articles.headline: US Buyers Seek Discounts from Indian Apparel Exporters Amid Peace Hopes
articles.summary: As US-Iran truce prospects brighten, US buyers have resumed enquiries with Indian apparel exporters but are demanding 5-10% discounts, pressuring exporter margins. India's readymade garment (RMG) exports declined 14.2% in May, marking the sixth consecutive monthly drop, primarily due to weaker US demand.

articles.headline: Realtors Expect Margins to Improve as Oil Prices Decline
articles.summary: The de-escalation in West Asia and falling crude oil prices are raising hopes for India's real estate, construction, and infrastructure sectors. Developers expect improved project margins, as construction costs had risen over 25% since global conflicts began. While positive, benefits will emerge gradually as lower oil prices reflect in domestic fuel costs.

articles.headline: Bollywood Slumps After Dhurandhar 2 Success, Multiple Films Flop
articles.summary: After the blockbuster 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge', Hindi cinema has seen seven consecutive box-office failures. Industry experts attribute the slump to outdated story formulas, fading star power, weak marketing, and makers' disconnect with audience tastes. Small-budget experimental films like 'Karuppu' and 'Haunted 3D' have shown success, highlighting the need for relevant content.

articles.headline: Editorial: Delinking Disinvestment from Fiscal Maths Welcome for PSUs
articles.summary: The editorial supports the government's delinking of PSU disinvestment from fiscal arithmetic. It suggests that regular, smaller stake sales are a better approach in volatile markets than large, calendar-based sales. The piece argues that a systematic withdrawal plan from business should maximize public money realization from PSUs and mitigate competitive/governance issues through continuous reforms.

articles.headline: Editorial: US Must Restrain Israel to Secure Iran Peace Deal
articles.summary: The editorial argues that the US must ensure Israel doesn't act with impunity to avoid derailing the Iran-US peace deal. It references Israel's recent strikes in Beirut and Trump's reported warnings to Netanyahu not to strike Beirut. The piece emphasizes Washington's credibility depends on its ability to deliver outcomes and restrain its allies to secure the deal.

articles.headline: India's Market Cap Crosses $5 Trillion Mark Again, Ranks Sixth Globally
articles.summary: India's total market capitalization surpassed $5 trillion again, reclaiming the sixth global spot, driven by a rebound after the US-Iran peace deal. The market cap stood at $5,003.43 billion on Wednesday, up from $4,864.90 billion on June 12. Taiwan and South Korea saw sharper gains from AI-linked companies, while FPIs pared exposure to Indian equities.

articles.headline: $26 Billion Shares Eligible for Sale as IPO Lock-ins Expire
articles.summary: About $26 billion worth of shares from 71 recently listed companies will become eligible for sale between June 17 and September-end as IPO lock-ins expire. This includes $15.96 billion across 31 companies in the next month. While sales aren't guaranteed, the prospect could create a supply overhang, potentially weighing on stock prices.

articles.headline: New Fed Chair Warsh Holds Rates, Sees Hike Later in 2026
articles.summary: The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady, but policymakers now project a hike by end-2026, dropping previous language signaling further reductions. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's influence was evident in the shortened policy statement, approved unanimously. Growing inflation concerns above the 2% target fueled the shift in outlook.

articles.headline: Nifty Tops 24,000 with Fourth Straight Day of Gains
articles.summary: Indian equity benchmarks extended gains for a fourth session, with Nifty 50 rising 0.4% to 24,085.70 and Sensex up 0.5% to 77,155.62. Easing West Asia crisis fears and moderating crude prices boosted sentiment. Analysts see promising near-term outlook, with large-caps offering favorable risk-reward, but mid-caps appear expensive.

articles.headline: Sebi Cautions Against Trades in Unlisted Public Company Securities
articles.summary: Sebi warned investors against buying or selling securities of unlisted public companies through unauthorized electronic platforms and websites. The regulator stated these platforms are neither authorized nor recognized by Sebi, urging caution due to associated risks and potential for sharing personal details on unregulated platforms.

articles.headline: RBI Removes Cap on NRI Deposit Rates Until September 30
articles.summary: The RBI temporarily removed the interest rate ceiling on non-resident deposits (FCNR-B and NRE accounts for 3+ years) until September 30, 2026. This allows banks to offer higher rates, potentially up to 8%, to mobilize overseas funds, strengthen asset liability management (ALM), and improve Liquidity Coverage Ratio. Banks had already raised FCNR-B rates by 250-450 bps.

articles.headline: Tata Electronics Onboards Malaysian Cleanroom Specialist for Dholera Fab
articles.summary: Tata Electronics partnered with Malaysian cleanroom specialist IAQ Group for its upcoming 300 mm semiconductor fab in Dholera. IAQ is renowned for expertise in hi-tech facility solutions, crucial for meeting stringent cleanroom standards required for semiconductor manufacturing. This partnership is vital amidst global AI-driven fab expansion.

articles.headline: Amazon May Face Billions in FTC Fine Over Advertiser Misleading Claims
articles.summary: Amazon may face a lawsuit from the US Federal Trade Commission (FTC) seeking civil penalties. Allegations suggest the e-commerce giant misled advertisers placing ads on its website. The FTC's probe is ongoing, with several state attorneys general also participating.

articles.headline: Estonia Plans to Grant Personal IDs and Rights to AI Bots
articles.summary: Estonia plans to assign personal identification numbers to AI assistants, giving them legal rights and holding them accountable for actions taken on behalf of businesses and individuals. This makes Estonia the first country globally to take such a step, addressing legal ramifications of rapid AI growth.

articles.headline: Rapido Early Backers Eye `100 Crore Share Sale in Secondaries
articles.summary: Early and angel investors in urban mobility startup Rapido are looking to sell shares worth `100 crore in a secondary transaction. This is part of a larger $730 million funding round. Sellers seek a $3 billion valuation, but prospective investors are pushing back for lower valuations. Swiggy has fully exited its stake in Rapido.

articles.headline: India's Electronics Exports Target Second Rank Globally
articles.summary: Electronics has emerged as India's third-largest export category, with the government aiming for it to become the second largest. IT Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw stated the domestic electronics manufacturing industry is worth `13 lakh crore. Exports rose 11.62% year-on-year in May to $5.09 billion, with smartphones making up 73.8% of this total.

articles.headline: Veterinary Care Startup Vetic Raises $40 Million in Funding
articles.summary: Gurugram-based veterinary care startup Vetic closed a $40 million funding round led by Bessemer Venture Partners, with participation from Greenoaks Capital, Lachy Groom, and JSW Family Office. The company is valued at $130-140 million post-money and plans to expand its network of clinics to 25 centers across key Indian cities within the next year.

articles.headline: Peak XV, Nexus Set for 7-9x Returns on Turtlemint IPO Shares
articles.summary: Peak XV Partners and Nexus Venture Partners are poised to achieve 7-9 times returns on shares sold in insurtech company Turtlemint's upcoming IPO. They will collectively hold over `1,600 crore worth of stock post-listing. Peak XV expects `66.2 crore from selling 4.4 million shares (7.2x return), while Nexus anticipates `41.8 crore from 2.8 million shares (8.8x return).

articles.headline: AI Startup Pramaana Labs Secures $27 Million Seed Funding
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence (AI) startup Pramaana Labs raised $27 million in a seed funding round led by Khosla Ventures, with participation from Accel, BoldCap, Nexus Venture Partners, Premji Invest, and Unbound. Founded in 2025 by IIT Madras alumni Ranjan Rajagopalan and Sanjay Ganapathy, the Palo Alto-based startup will use the funds to expand its AI research staff and formalization models.

articles.headline: IT Firms Struggle to Find AI-Savvy Engineers, Use Multiple Specialists
articles.summary: Indian IT companies are struggling to attract forward deployed engineers (FDEs) who combine deep tech expertise with customer-facing skills, crucial for AI adoption. They are improvising by using multiple specialists, bundling different roles as a single FDE to reduce cost, leading to clients not always getting the value they expect.

articles.headline: India Key to Mercedes-Benz's Tech Stack and Autonomous Driving
articles.summary: Mercedes-Benz CTO Jörg Burzer stated India is crucial to the automaker's technology stack and autonomous-driving plans, with its Bengaluru R&D center playing a significant role. The center contributed to the MB.OS proprietary operating system and is vital for Level 2++ and future Level 3/4 autonomous driving.

articles.headline: JLR Projects Revenue Growth & Profitability, Market Unimpressed
articles.summary: Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) projected a turnaround for FY27, guiding for 13% revenue growth to £26 billion (from £23 billion in FY26) and an EBIT margin of around 4%. Operating cash flow is expected to breakeven. However, the market was unimpressed, with Tata Motors PV shares plunging 8.27%, as analysts found the 4% EBIT margin guidance weaker than estimated.

articles.headline: NARCL Recovers `575 Crore from Agson Global, Marks Fifth Exit
articles.summary: National Asset Reconstruction Company Ltd (NARCL) recovered over `575 crore from the resolution of aroma ingredients manufacturer Agson Global, marking its fifth successful exit from a stressed-loan account. NARCL, the state-backed "bad bank," fully redeemed security receipts issued to lenders and distributed an additional `200 crore to banks.

articles.headline: HDFC Bank Raises $750 Million ECB Under RBI Special Swap Plan
articles.summary: HDFC Bank was the first to use RBI's 1.5% fixed rate swap, raising $750 million via 5-year bonds through its GIFT City IFSC Banking Unit. This is the bank’s first overseas issue since Feb 2024, priced at a tight 90 basis points over US treasury. The RBI swap arrangement removes the need for banks to hedge future dollar liabilities, saving up to 4% on hedging costs.

articles.headline: FPIs Pump Record `33,000 Crore into G-Secs in June
articles.summary: Foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) injected a record `33,000 crore into government bond markets in June so far, six times the May figure. This surge follows tax exemptions on interest and capital gains, removal of investment limits, and a calmer geopolitical environment. The inflows indicate increased investor confidence, but sustained momentum depends on several factors.

articles.headline: JBM Ecolife Mobility Raises `900 Crore for Electric Bus Expansion
articles.summary: JBM Ecolife Mobility, JBM Auto's electric mobility platform, raised `900 crore from Motilal Oswal Private Credit. The funding, comprising `450 crore structured debt and `450 crore equity-linked capital, will support the expansion of its electric bus business. The capital infusion is expected to scale its electric bus platform to nearly 5,000 buses from 2,000 currently.

articles.headline: Microfinance Access Tough for First-Timers Amid Tightened Norms
articles.summary: Credit access for first-time borrowers in microfinance has tightened, with their share falling to 20% in March from 33% three years ago. Lenders are focusing on borrowers with established credit histories due to past asset quality strain. About 23 million small borrowers exited the formal banking system, as average ticket sizes increased to `62,945.

articles.headline: Rupee Gains Marginally but Remains Below Pre-Iran War Level
articles.summary: The rupee marginally gained on Wednesday, closing at 94.53 against the dollar, retreating from its six-week high of 94.29 seen earlier. Despite this, it remains sharply weaker than its pre-Iran war level of 90.98. Anticipation of rising dollar flows into Indian banks, spurred by RBI measures and softening crude prices, is expected to support further gains.

articles.headline: Defence Stocks Rally on Record Output and Growth Promise
articles.summary: Defence stocks surged 5-19% after the government announced India's defence production hit a record `1.78 lakh crore in FY26, a 15.6% increase. Paras Defence and Hindustan Aeronautics led gains. Analysts are bullish due to strong policy push, rising global demand post-Ukraine war, and India's low-cost sourcing base, creating new opportunities.

articles.headline: Jill Biden Memoir Reflects on White House Years, Criticisms
articles.summary: In a new memoir, former US First Lady Jill Biden reflects on her time in the White House, defending Joe Biden's reelection decision and addressing criticisms. She describes her grief over the East Wing demolition, questions about her husband's health, and awkward interactions with Melania Trump, expressing frustration over fashion scrutiny.

articles.headline: Anish Kapoor Returns to Hayward Gallery with Bold New Works
articles.summary: Artist Anish Kapoor presented bold, large new works and older sculptures at London's Hayward Gallery, 28 years after his first UK solo show there. The exhibition features shiny mirrored steel and void-like black sculptures, visceral paintings, and large-scale installations, including 'Mount Moriah at the Gate of the Ghetto'.

articles.headline: Naomi Campbell Appeals Charity Trustee Ban, Alleges Deception
articles.summary: Supermodel Naomi Campbell appealed a five-year ban as a charity trustee, telling a tribunal she was "deceived" by fellow trustee Bianka Hellmich, who allegedly forged her signature. The Charity Commission disqualified Campbell for financial mismanagement, citing thousands of pounds used for luxury hotel stays and spa treatments from charity funds. Campbell denies personal gain.

articles.headline: Formula One Reports 35% Reduction in Carbon Footprint Since 2018
articles.summary: Formula One achieved a 35% reduction in its carbon footprint since 2018, reporting a 12% drop in 2024 alone. The motor racing organization remains on track to become net-zero carbon by 2030. Travel emissions also saw a 27% reduction, with teams investing in sustainable aviation fuel.

articles.headline: EU Chief Seeks Back Channel with Putin to End Ukraine War
articles.summary: European Council President Antonio Costa contacted the Kremlin to engage Vladimir Putin in discussions to end the Ukraine war. Costa's adviser held two calls with a senior Russian official to prepare for substantive talks. Some EU countries propose a special envoy, while others see an opportunity as Russian forces struggle and economic costs mount.

articles.headline: US Retail Sales Jumped 0.9% in May, Driven by Auto Purchases and Gasoline Prices
articles.summary: US retail sales increased 0.9% in May, exceeding expectations, with households boosting motor vehicle purchases. Sales at gasoline stations accelerated 3.4% as prices surged to four-year highs due to the West Asia war. Despite economic resilience, a slowdown is anticipated as tax refunds diminish and higher prices persist.

articles.headline: G7 Hails Unified US Approach on Pressure Against Russia
articles.summary: G7 leaders praised a newly unified approach to increase pressure on Russia to end the Ukraine war, sensing a shift by President Trump to a tougher stance. They agreed to strengthen sanctions, including on Moscow's fossil fuel revenues. French President Macron noted a "very deep change" in the US approach, acknowledging Russia's disinterest in peace.

articles.headline: Washington's Oil Exports to Cuba Jump Dramatically Through Loophole
articles.summary: US exports to Cuba, primarily fuel, appliances, and vehicles, have surged dramatically this year, tripling through mid-May compared to all of 2025. Businesses exploit a "Support for the Cuban People" trade loophole, allowing exports without cumbersome licences. This shift began in February when Cuba allowed private imports, bypassing Trump's sanctions.

articles.headline: Oil Prices Rise 1% Amid IEA Warning of Future Supply Glut
articles.summary: Oil prices rose over 1% on Wednesday, recovering from three-month lows, after Donald Trump threatened to resume bombing Iran. However, the International Energy Agency (IEA) warned of a significant supply overhang in 2027, projecting global supply to surge by 8 million barrels per day while demand rises by only 2 million bpd.

articles.headline: Green Economy Market Cap Tops $10 Trillion Globally
articles.summary: The global green economy, comprising companies generating revenue from climate solutions, reached a record $10 trillion market value. Revenues from environmental products and services climbed to $5.5 trillion last year. Companies deriving over 20% of income from green activities outperformed the broader equity market, with the S&P Global Clean Energy Transition Index surging 80% since 2024.

articles.headline: Egypt's Pound Becomes World's Top-performing Currency
articles.summary: Egypt's pound became the world's best-performing currency, rising about 4% against the dollar since Friday, following a US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. This is expected to restore supply flows and ease inflation concerns. The pound strengthened past 50 per dollar and is up more than 7% since early May.

articles.headline: भारत-ईयू मुक्त व्यापार समझौता 15 जुलाई 2026 से होगा लागू
articles.summary: भारत और यूरोपीय संघ (ईयू) के बीच मुक्त व्यापार समझौता (एफटीए) 15 जुलाई 2026 से प्रभावी होगा। वाणिज्य मंत्रालय ने यह जानकारी दी। समझौते पर 24 जुलाई, 2025 को हस्ताक्षर हुए थे। यह वैश्विक आर्थिक भागीदारी को मजबूत करने की दिशा में एक महत्वपूर्ण कदम है।

articles.headline: पीएम मोदी और ट्रंप की मुलाकात: व्यापार करार पर जल्द सहमति की उम्मीद
articles.summary: प्रधानमंत्री नरेंद्र मोदी और अमेरिकी राष्ट्रपति डोनाल्ड ट्रंप ने जी7 शिखर सम्मेलन से इतर द्विपक्षीय बैठक की। ट्रंप ने कहा कि भारत और अमेरिका व्यापार समझौते के बहुत करीब हैं। मोदी ने भारतीय नाविकों की सुरक्षा और संरक्षा का मुद्दा उठाया। यह 16 महीनों में दोनों नेताओं की पहली मुलाकात थी, जिसमें रिश्तों में सुधार के सकारात्मक संकेत मिले।

articles.headline: पश्चिम एशिया संकट के बावजूद भारत को विदेशी धन प्रेषण में वृद्धि
articles.summary: पश्चिम एशिया संकट के बावजूद भारतीय प्रवासी लगातार देश में धन भेज रहे हैं। वित्तीय वर्ष 2026 में विदेश से धन प्रेषण 144 अरब डॉलर के पार पहुंचने का अनुमान है, जो पिछले वर्ष 124.55 अरब डॉलर से अधिक है। अमेरिका धन प्रेषण का सबसे बड़ा स्रोत बना हुआ है, जिसकी हिस्सेदारी 27.7% है।

articles.headline: शेयर बाजार में लगातार चौथे दिन तेजी, सेंसेक्स 347 अंक चढ़ा
articles.summary: अमेरिकी-ईरान शांति समझौते की घोषणा और कच्चे तेल की कीमतों में नरमी से निवेशकों की धारणा सुधरी। बुधवार को घरेलू शेयर बाजार लगातार चौथे कारोबारी सत्र में बढ़त के साथ बंद हुए। सेंसेक्स 347.14 अंक चढ़कर 77,155.62 पर बंद हुआ, जबकि निफ्टी 96.55 अंक की बढ़त के साथ 24,085.70 पर बंद हुआ।

articles.headline: एचडीएफसी बैंक ने बॉन्ड से 75 करोड़ डॉलर जुटाए
articles.summary: भारत के निजी क्षेत्र के सबसे बड़े ऋणदाता एचडीएफसी बैंक ने 5 साल के सीनियर सुरक्षित बॉन्ड निर्गम के माध्यम से 75 करोड़ डॉलर जुटाए। यह इस साल किसी भारतीय बैंक द्वारा विदेश से जुटाई गई सबसे बड़ी धनराशि है। बॉन्ड पर 5.067% की निश्चित ब्याज दर है और ये 24 जून 2031 को परिपक्व होंगे।

articles.headline: नीट-यूजी पेपर लीक: दिल्ली HC ने टेलीग्राम प्रतिबंध पर केंद्र से जवाब मांगा
articles.summary: दिल्ली उच्च न्यायालय ने केंद्र सरकार को मैसेजिंग ऐप टेलीग्राम पर लगाए गए अस्थायी प्रतिबंध के खिलाफ दायर याचिका पर जवाब दाखिल करने को कहा। यह प्रतिबंध 21 जून को होने वाली नीट-यूजी 2026 पुन परीक्षा से पहले लगाया गया था। टेलीग्राम का आरोप है कि प्रतिबंध गैरकानूनी है और 15 करोड़ से अधिक उपयोगकर्ता प्रभावित हुए हैं।

articles.headline: पीएम ई-ड्राइव योजना समाप्ति के करीब, बढ़ सकते हैं ई-दोपहिया के दाम
articles.summary: प्रमुख इलेक्ट्रिक दोपहिया निर्माता पीएम ई-ड्राइव सब्सिडी योजना के समापन की तैयारी कर रहे हैं। योजना के लिए आवंटित राशि जल्द खत्म होने वाली है, जिससे ई-दोपहिया के दाम बढ़ सकते हैं। 24.79 लाख ई-दोपहिया के लक्ष्य के मुकाबले लगभग 23.29 लाख वाहनों को पहले ही सब्सिडी मिल चुकी है।

articles.headline: मुंबई में पानी की कमी से निर्माण परियोजनाओं पर ब्रेक लगने की आशंका
articles.summary: मुंबई के जलाशयों में घटते जल स्तर और मानसून में देरी के कारण बीएमसी ने निर्माण कार्यों के लिए जल आपूर्ति पर रोक लगा दी है। इससे निर्माण लागत बढ़ने और परियोजनाओं की समयसीमा में देरी होने की चिंता बढ़ गई है। रियल एस्टेट फर्मों के शेयर में 0.35% से 2.54% की गिरावट दर्ज की गई।

articles.headline: एचएफसीएल को भारतनेट चरण-3 परियोजना के लिए 2,666 करोड़ का ठेका
articles.summary: दूरसंचार कंपनी एचएफसीएल को भारतनेट चरण-3 परियोजना के तहत उपकरणों की आपूर्ति के लिए रेल विकास निगम लिमिटेड (आरवीएनएल) से 2,666 करोड़ रुपये का ठेका मिला है। इसमें दूरसंचार उपकरणों की आपूर्ति, उन्हें लगाने और चालू करने का कार्य शामिल है।

articles.headline: ऑल्टियस इनविट में 7.3% हिस्सेदारी 3,656 करोड़ रुपये में बेची गई
articles.summary: बुकफील्ड, ब्रिटिश कोलंबिया इन्वेस्टमेंट मैनेजमेंट कॉर्पोरेशन (बीसीआई) और सिंगापुर के संप्रभु धन कोष जीआईसी ने खुले बाजार सौदों के माध्यम से ऑल्टियस टेलीकॉम इन्फ्रास्ट्रक्चर ट्रस्ट (ऑल्टियस इनविट) में 7.3% हिस्सेदारी 3,656 करोड़ रुपये में बेच दी। यह लगभग 22.15 करोड़ यूनिट्स की बिक्री थी।

articles.headline: मजबूत बिक्री से फार्मा बाजार में संतुलन और वृद्धि के संकेत
articles.summary: भारत के दवा बाजार में संतुलित वृद्धि दिख रही है, जिसमें बिक्री, कीमतों में इजाफा और नई दवाओं के बाजार में आने का अहम योगदान है। मई 2026 में भारतीय दवा बाजार (आईपीएम) की 10.7% की वार्षिक कुल वृद्धि दर दर्ज की गई। नए उपचारों, विशेष रूप से जीएलपी-1 एगोनिस्ट, ने बाजार विस्तार में महत्वपूर्ण भूमिका निभाई है।

articles.headline: डीएस ग्रुप की मैरियट के साथ होटल निर्माण के लिए साझेदारी
articles.summary: कैच ब्रांड के मसाले बनाने वाले धर्मपाल सत्यपाल ग्रुप (डीएस ग्रुप) ने दिल्ली में डब्ल्यू ब्रांड के होटलों की शुरुआत करने के लिए मैरियट इंटरनेशनल के साथ साझेदारी की है। समूह एयरोसिटी में 200 कमरों वाले लग्जरी होटल के निर्माण में 400 करोड़ रुपये का निवेश करेगा।

articles.headline: एरिक्सन इंडिया: पुर्जों की कीमतों में भारी वृद्धि, आपूर्ति श्रृंखला में बाधाएं
articles.summary: एरिक्सन इंडिया के प्रबंध निदेशक नितिन बंसल ने कहा कि पुर्जों, खासकर सेमीकंडक्टर चिप्स की कीमतों में भारी वृद्धि और मांग की तुलना में सीमित उत्पादन के साथ-साथ पश्चिम एशिया के युद्ध के कारण आपूर्ति श्रृंखला में बाधाएं आई हैं। इससे कंपनी को भविष्य के लिए योजना बनाने की अवधि बढ़ानी पड़ी है।

articles.headline: एसीसी लिमिटेड का उत्पादन क्षमता विस्तार रहेगा जारी: करण अदाणी
articles.summary: एसीसी लिमिटेड के चेयरमैन करण अदाणी ने कहा कि कंपनी उत्पादन क्षमता का संतुलित ढंग से विस्तार जारी रखने के साथ रेडी-मिक्स कंक्रीट कारोबार को मजबूत करने और कम उत्सर्जन वाली प्रौद्योगिकियों को अपनाने पर जोर देगी। भारतीय अर्थव्यवस्था का दीर्घकालिक परिदृश्य मजबूत है।

articles.headline: कोफोर्ज का राजस्व दोगुना करने की योजना, एआई पर निर्भरता
articles.summary: आईटी कंपनी कोफोर्ज अगले चार साल में अपना राजस्व दोगुना कर 5 अरब डॉलर करने की योजना बना रही है, जिसमें एनकोरा के अधिग्रहण को भी शामिल किया जाएगा। कंपनी एआई समाधानों का उपयोग करके विकास की रफ्तार बढ़ाने और कामकाज बेहतर बनाने पर ध्यान केंद्रित कर रही है।

articles.headline: आरआईएल की एजीएम में जियो IPO और डेटा सेंटर रणनीति पर नजर
articles.summary: रिलायंस इंडस्ट्रीज (आरआईएल) की 49वीं सालाना आम बैठक (एजीएम) में बाजार की नजर रिलायंस जियो इनफोकॉम के आईपीओ और एआई तथा डेटा सेंटर पर कंपनी की रणनीति पर रहेगी। विश्लेषकों का मानना है कि जियो इंफोकॉम जल्द ही अपने संभावित 4 अरब डॉलर के आईपीओ के लिए मसौदा पत्र जमा कर सकती है।

articles.headline: वित्तीय विवरणों में पारदर्शिता के लिए सीएफओ जिम्मेदार: एनएफआरए अध्यक्ष
articles.summary: नेशनल फाइनेंशियल रिपोर्टिंग अथॉरिटी (एनएफआरए) के अध्यक्ष नितिन गुप्ता ने कहा कि कंपनियों के वित्तीय विवरण तैयार करने के लिए मुख्य वित्तीय अधिकारी (सीएफओ) जिम्मेदार होते हैं और उन्हें वित्तीय स्थिति को पारदर्शी तरीके से पेश करना चाहिए। उन्होंने राजेश एक्सपोर्ट्स मामले का जिक्र किया।

articles.headline: बीएसई का बाजार पूंजीकरण पहली बार $5 लाख करोड़ के पार
articles.summary: बुधवार को बीएसई में सूचीबद्ध सभी कंपनियों का कुल बाजार पूंजीकरण पहली बार $5 लाख करोड़ के पार पहुंच गया। यह कच्चे तेल की कीमतों में गिरावट और अमेरिका-ईरान शांति समझौते की घोषणा से निवेशकों की धारणा में सुधार के कारण हुआ। मझोली कंपनियों का बीएसई मिडकैप सूचकांक 1.20% चढ़ा।

articles.headline: सेबी ने अनाधिकृत ऑनलाइन शेयर ट्रेडिंग प्लेटफॉर्म पर निवेशकों को चेताया
articles.summary: भारतीय प्रतिभूति एवं विनिमय बोर्ड (सेबी) ने अनाधिकृत ऑनलाइन मंचों या वेबसाइटों के जरिए गैर-सूचीबद्ध सार्वजनिक हिस्सेदारी वाली कंपनियों के शेयरों की खरीद-बिक्री को लेकर निवेशकों को आगाह किया। सेबी ने कहा कि ऐसे मंच मान्यता प्राप्त नहीं हैं और न ही नियामक के अधीन आते हैं, जिससे निवेशकों को बड़ा जोखिम हो सकता है।

articles.headline: भारतीय माइक्रोफाइनेंस क्षेत्र में सुधार के शुरुआती संकेत
articles.summary: मार्च 2025 से मार्च 2026 के बीच सालाना आधार पर पोर्टफोलियो में 17% गिरावट के बाद भारतीय माइक्रोफाइनेंस क्षेत्र में सुधार के शुरुआती संकेत दिख रहे हैं। जनवरी से मार्च 2026 की अवधि में उद्योग के पोर्टफोलियो में तिमाही आधार पर 3% वृद्धि हुई, जिससे लगातार 4 तिमाहियों की गिरावट का सिलसिला टूटा।

articles.headline: भारत में शिपिंग बीमा क्लब बनाने की संभावना
articles.summary: समुद्री नियामक द्वारा कराए गए अध्ययन में पाया गया है कि एक भारतीय प्रोटेक्शन और इंडेमनिटी (P&I) क्लब व्यवहार्य है, भले ही यह फिक्स्ड प्रीमियम मॉडल पर आधारित हो। यह कदम विदेशी बीमा कंपनियों को दिए जाने वाले अरबों डॉलर बचाने में मदद कर सकता है।

articles.headline: वित्त वर्ष 2026 में रक्षा उत्पादन रिकॉर्ड 1.78 लाख करोड़ रुपये
articles.summary: वित्तीय वर्ष 2026 में भारत के रक्षा क्षेत्र का उत्पादन सालाना आधार पर लगभग 16% बढ़कर 1.78 लाख करोड़ रुपये हो गया। यह देश के 3 लाख करोड़ रुपये के लक्ष्य के आधे से अधिक है। निजी क्षेत्र की हिस्सेदारी भी बढ़कर एक चौथाई के करीब पहुंच गई।

articles.headline: कृषि मंत्रालय को अल नीनो पूर्वानुमान का इंतजार
articles.summary: कृषि सचिव अतीश चंद्रा ने कहा कि कृषि मंत्रालय अल नीनो के संभावित असर को कम करने के लिए ठोस कदम तय करने से पहले इस महीने के अंत तक मौसम विभाग के पूर्वानुमान का इंतजार कर रहा है। इससे अल नीनो के आगमन की समय-सीमा स्पष्ट हो सकेगी।

articles.headline: हितेश जोशी जीआईसी रे के नए सीएमडी नियुक्त
articles.summary: सार्वजनिक क्षेत्र की पुनर्बीमा कंपनी जनरल इंश्योरेंस कॉर्पोरेशन ऑफ इंडिया (जीआईसी रे) ने हितेश रमेश चंद जोशी को नया चेयरमैन एवं प्रबंध निदेशक (सीएमडी) नियुक्त करने की घोषणा की। जोशी ने 16 जून को आधिकारिक तौर पर अपना कार्यभार संभाल लिया है।

articles.headline: भारत को नवीकरणीय ऊर्जा के लिए 10 गीगावाट/घंटे बैटरी स्टोरेज की जरूरत
articles.summary: एम्बर थिंक टैंक के विश्लेषण के अनुसार, भारत को नवीकरणीय ऊर्जा की बर्बादी को रोकने के लिए लगभग 10 गीगावाट प्रति घंटे की बैटरी ऊर्जा भंडारण प्रणाली की आवश्यकता है। वित्तीय वर्ष 2026 में कोयले को उसके न्यूनतम तकनीकी भार से ऊपर रखने के कारण नवीकरणीय ऊर्जा उत्पादन में 2.1 टेरावाट-घंटे की कटौती हुई।

articles.headline: केंद्र न्यूनतम श्रम संहिता लागू करने के लिए डिजिटल आधार बना रहा
articles.summary: श्रम और रोजगार मंत्रालय ने राज्यों से आवश्यक बुनियादी ढांचे के निर्माण और समाधान पोर्टलों को केंद्रीय श्रम सुविधा पोर्टल से जोड़ने को कहा है। यह चार श्रम संहिताओं को लागू करने की दिशा में एक महत्वपूर्ण कदम है, जिसका उद्देश्य देश में श्रम प्रशासन के लिए एक एकीकृत डिजिटल पारिस्थितिकी तंत्र बनाना है।

articles.headline: कोयले से गैस बनाने की योजना पर मुंबई में तीसरा रोडशो
articles.summary: कोयला मंत्रालय 18 जून को मुंबई में 46,000 करोड़ रुपये की सतह कोयला और लिग्नाइट गैसीकरण कार्यक्रम में उद्योग की भागीदारी को आकर्षित करने के लिए एक रोडशो का आयोजन करेगा। यह रसायन और औद्योगिक फीडस्टॉक के उत्पादन के लिए घरेलू कोयले के उपयोग को बढ़ावा देने के प्रयासों का हिस्सा है।

articles.headline: आरबीआई ने बैंकिंग प्रणाली में 72,300 करोड़ रुपये डाले
articles.summary: भारतीय रिजर्व बैंक ने बुधवार को बैंकिंग प्रणाली में नकदी बनाए रखने के लिए 72,300 करोड़ रुपये डाले। अग्रिम कर भुगतान के कारण बाजार में नकदी का अधिशेष घट जाने पर यह राशि दो परिवर्तनीय रीपो दर (वीआरआर) नीलामियों के जरिए डाली गई।

articles.headline: भारत को लंबे समय तक ऊर्जा की दिक्कतों का सामना करना पड़ेगा
articles.summary: रिपोर्ट के अनुसार, भारत को लंबे समय तक ऊर्जा की दिक्कतों का सामना करना पड़ेगा, जिसका असर उद्योग पर पड़ेगा, विशेषकर विनिर्माण और निर्माण क्षेत्रों पर। इससे जीडीपी वृद्धि वित्तीय वर्ष 2027 में सुस्त होकर 6.6% रहने का अनुमान है। भू-राजनीतिक तनाव कम होने के बावजूद आपूर्ति सामान्य होने में समय लगेगा।

articles.headline: केंद्रीय गृह मंत्री और मणिपुर राज्यपाल ने राज्य की स्थिति पर चर्चा की
articles.summary: केंद्रीय गृह मंत्री अमित शाह और मणिपुर के राज्यपाल अजय कुमार भल्ला ने बुधवार को राज्य की स्थिति पर चर्चा की। जातीय समूहों के बीच सोमवार को हुई गोलीबारी में तीन यूथ घायल होने के बाद राज्य में विरोध प्रदर्शन हुए थे।

articles.headline: भारत-ईयू व्यापार सौदा साल के अंत तक, निवेश समझौतों पर तेजी
articles.summary: यूरोपीय आयोग की अध्यक्ष उर्सुला वॉन डेर लेयेन ने कहा कि भारत और यूरोपीय संघ के बीच व्यापक व्यापार समझौते पर इस साल के अंत तक औपचारिक रूप से हस्ताक्षर हो जाएंगे। दोनों पक्ष निवेश समझौते पर तेजी से काम करेंगे। यह घोषणा जी7 शिखर सम्मेलन के दौरान प्रधानमंत्री मोदी के साथ मुलाकात के बाद हुई।

articles.headline: नवी मुंबई एयरपोर्ट से हर माह 10 लाख यात्री उड़ान भरेंगे
articles.summary: अदाणी समूह द्वारा संचालित नवी मुंबई अंतर्राष्ट्रीय हवाई अड्डे ने आगामी नवंबर से हर महीने 10 लाख से अधिक यात्रियों की आवाजाही का लक्ष्य निर्धारित किया है। यदि यह लक्ष्य हासिल होता है, तो यह देश का आठवां हवाई अड्डा बन जाएगा। हवाई अड्डा विमानों की आवाजाही को रोजाना 150 से बढ़ाकर 250 करेगा।

articles.headline: आरएसएस प्रमुख भागवत: भारत का उत्थान रोकने के लिए झूठे विमर्श
articles.summary: आरएसएस प्रमुख मोहन भागवत ने बुधवार को कहा कि भारत का इतिहास गुलामी का नहीं, बल्कि आक्रमणकारियों के खिलाफ निरंतर प्रतिरोध का है। उन्होंने आरोप लगाया कि देश और विदेश में भारत के उत्थान को रोकने के लिए झूठे विमर्श गढ़े जा रहे हैं।

articles.headline: लोकसभा अध्यक्ष ममता गुट के बागी सांसदों का पक्ष सुनेंगे
articles.summary: लोकसभा अध्यक्ष ओम बिरला ने तृणमूल कांग्रेस नेता अभिषेक बनर्जी को पार्टी में विभाजन के मुद्दे पर अपना पक्ष रखने के लिए 19 जून को बैठक के लिए आमंत्रित किया है। यह तब हुआ जब तृणमूल के 20 बागी सांसदों ने नेशनलिस्ट सिटिजंस पार्टी ऑफ इंडिया (एनसीपीआई) में विलय कर स्वयं को अलग समूह के रूप में मान्यता देने की मांग की है।

articles.headline: नेपाल भारत और चीन से मजबूत संबंध चाहता है: विदेश मंत्री खनाल
articles.summary: नेपाल के विदेश मंत्री शिशिर खनाल ने कहा कि नेपाल सरकार भारत और चीन के साथ मजबूत संबंध बनाना चाहती है। उन्होंने चीन के विदेश मंत्री वांग यी और वरिष्ठ नेताओं के साथ व्यापक बातचीत की। यह यात्रा उनकी भारत यात्रा के तुरंत बाद हुई, जिसमें उन्होंने भारत के साथ संबंधों को सर्वोच्च प्राथमिकता देने की बात कही थी।

articles.headline: Iran-U.S. framework deal outlines nuclear pledge, financial relief
articles.summary: A 14-point framework agreement between the U.S. and Iran, to be signed in Geneva, details Iran's pledge against nuclear weapons and the U.S.'s commitment to facilitate the release of frozen Iranian assets and provide $300 billion for economic development. The deal also involves Iran diluting enriched uranium and calls for an immediate end to the war.

articles.headline: Odisha CM orders action over errors in textbooks
articles.summary: Odisha Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi ordered immediate corrective measures and action against officials responsible for 1,678 errors found in Class 1 to 8 school textbooks. Mistakes included describing Newton as a 'greatest pilot' and using a Karnataka Assembly photo for Odisha. A three-member panel will investigate, and schools reopen Thursday.

articles.headline: L-G orders citywide audit of schools for POCSO compliance
articles.summary: Delhi L-G Taranjit Singh Sandhu ordered a comprehensive audit of all city schools to assess compliance with the POCSO Act. He stressed a 'zero-tolerance approach' to harassment and molestation, directing police to maintain a visible presence near schools and conduct a month-long child protection awareness campaign in July. Every school must have a Child Protection Committee.

articles.headline: SC seeks Delhi Police reply on bail pleas of two ‘Indian Mujahideen operatives’
articles.summary: The Supreme Court asked Delhi Police to respond to bail pleas from two alleged Indian Mujahideen operatives incarcerated for 12 years. The court noted its Najeeb judgment (on prolonged incarceration) would apply, despite a later larger Bench referral concerning Umar Khalid and Sharjeel Imam's bail. The Delhi HC had previously denied their bail.

articles.headline: Five clusters identified under new slum rehabilitation policy
articles.summary: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta announced five clusters in Mayur Vihar, Seelampur, Sultanpuri, Lajpat Nagar, and Pitampura have been identified under the new Delhi Slum and JJ Cluster Rehabilitation and Relocation Policy, 2026. Tendering for infrastructure projects providing basic amenities and dignified housing will begin within 45 days, to provide dignified housing to slum residents.

articles.headline: Two held for firing outside Guru Randhawa-linked gym
articles.summary: Two alleged associates of the Lawrence Bishnoi gang, Arman (19) and Tushar alias Tashu (21), were arrested for firing outside a gym linked to Punjabi singer Guru Randhawa in Paschim Vihar on June 11. No injuries were reported. Gangster Anil Pandit allegedly claimed responsibility on social media. The Crime Branch tracked suspects across 100 km.

articles.headline: Won’t tolerate negligence during monsoon: CM to officials
articles.summary: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta warned officials against negligence during the monsoon season, directing all departments to monitor waterlogging-prone areas continuously. Nodal officers will be appointed for each point, held directly accountable for any waterlogging. She shared the 'Flood Control Order-2026' outlining guidelines on drainage, embankments, and control room contacts.

articles.headline: Govt. to organise three-day welfare camps to resolve citizen grievances
articles.summary: The Delhi government will hold three-day public welfare camps at 42 locations from Thursday, bringing various Central and Delhi government schemes and services to citizens. Chief Minister Rekha Gupta stated the aim is to ensure timely, transparent benefits and resolve grievances on the spot. Most camps will be in schools, functioning 10 a.m. to 5 p.m.

articles.headline: Speaker, Ministers take part in cleanliness drives
articles.summary: Delhi Assembly Speaker Vijender Gupta, Environment Minister Manjinder Singh Sirsa, and Labour Minister Kapil Mishra participated in cleanliness drives in Chandni Chowk and Model Town on Wednesday, marking 12 years of the Union government under PM Narendra Modi. Mr. Gupta urged citizens to make cleanliness a daily habit, while Mr. Sirsa emphasized collective responsibility.

articles.headline: Thapa resigns as GTA chief
articles.summary: Anit Thapa resigned as Chief Executive of the Gorkhaland Territorial Administration (GTA) on Wednesday, a day after CM Suvendu Adhikari visited Darjeeling hills and raised 'corruption' issues in the GTA. Thapa's party, Bharatiya Gorkha Prajatantrik Morcha (BGPM), an ally of Trinamool Congress, won 27 of 45 seats in the 2022 GTA elections.

articles.headline: Mamata marches against eviction of hawkers in W.B.
articles.summary: TMC chairperson Mamata Banerjee led a protest march in Kolkata against the eviction of hawkers, calling the drive 'illegal, unjust, and inhumane'. The CPI(M) also demanded rehabilitation before eviction. Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari defended the drive as addressing 'unchecked encroachment' rather than merely a livelihood issue, in what he termed 'anti-people policies'.

articles.headline: UCC Bill to be tabled in Monsoon Session of M.P. Assembly in July, says CM
articles.summary: Madhya Pradesh Chief Minister Mohan Yadav announced that a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Bill would be introduced and passed in the upcoming Monsoon Session of the Legislative Assembly, from July 20-24. A six-member high-level committee is drafting the Bill, inviting public suggestions and studying models from Uttarakhand, Gujarat, and Assam.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi speaks to family of U.P. sailor killed in U.S. attack
articles.summary: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi spoke to the family of Shivanand Chaurasiya, a U.P. sailor killed in a recent U.S. attack on an oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman, assuring them of all necessary help. The family demands a government job for the sister-in-law, criticizing State government officials for not meeting them.

articles.headline: Oppn. attacks Mann over video; BJP seeks FIR
articles.summary: Opposition parties in Punjab attacked Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann over an alleged objectionable video, with BJP leader Jagmohan Singh Raju demanding an FIR. Akal Takht Jathedar found the video 'authentic'. Critics accuse Mann of misleading the public, while Mann's team claims it is fake and AI-generated. Congress also criticized him for tarnishing Akal Takht's glory.

articles.headline: Parliamentary body meet convened by Sena (UBT) today amid split rumours
articles.summary: Shiv Sena (UBT) convened a parliamentary board meeting amid rumors of at least six of its nine Lok Sabha MPs rebelling. The party issued a whip and met LS Speaker Om Birla, requesting to be heard first if any other group approaches him, claiming rebel MPs were offered ₹50 crore each. Congress accused BJP of 'wrecking Indian democracy'.

articles.headline: Disqualification case: ex-AIADMK MLAs file responses to Speaker
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu Assembly Speaker J.C.D. Prabhakar said four former AIADMK MLAs, facing disqualification proceedings for defying the party whip and voting for the government, have submitted written responses. The MLAs later resigned and joined the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK). AIADMK whip seeks 'meaningful inquiry' before resignation acceptance, challenging gazette notifications.

articles.headline: 6 killed, 20 injured as bus crashes into truck in Vadodara
articles.summary: Six people were killed and 20 injured when a private bus rammed into a parked truck on the Vadodara-Halol highway in Gujarat early Wednesday. The luxury bus was traveling from Rajasthan to Surat. The truck had been parked on the roadside as its driver was checking tyre pressure. Majority of passengers were from Rajasthan.

articles.headline: Frenzied political activity as Jharkhand BJP seeks action over attack on ally RSS office in Ranchi
articles.summary: Jharkhand witnessed intense political activity before Rajya Sabha elections. BJP leaders questioned law and order after two unidentified persons allegedly hurled petrol bombs at the RSS office in Ranchi, describing it as an 'attack on a mother'. Police are investigating, with no arrests or motive confirmed. A nearby restaurant is being investigated.

articles.headline: War of words erupts as BJP ally Rajbhar says SP headed for split; Akhilesh hits back
articles.summary: A war of words erupted in Uttar Pradesh after SBSP president Om Prakash Rajbhar claimed the Samajwadi Party (SP) faces a major split, with many leaders joining BJP, citing Ram Gopal Yadav's letter to Amit Shah. SP chief Akhilesh Yadav countered, alleging BJP MLAs are ready to switch, and dismissed Rajbhar's claims as an attempt to divert attention.

articles.headline: Summer rush: police adopt new strategy as 15 lakh vehicles enter Shimla in 45 days
articles.summary: Shimla police adopted a five-point strategy to manage summer tourist rush, as 15 lakh vehicles entered between May 1 and June 15. Measures include additional force, surveillance, alternative routes, and public transport promotion, to tackle frequent traffic jams caused by heavy influx and malfunctioning vehicles. Occupancy at tourist accommodations was 60-90%.

articles.headline: Congress charts plan to emerge as AAP’s main challenger in Punjab Assembly poll
articles.summary: The Indian National Congress is formulating an electoral strategy to challenge the ruling Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) in the upcoming Punjab Assembly election. A three-member panel assessed the political situation, met senior leaders, and discussed a change in top leadership for the Punjab Pradesh Congress Committee to address internal discord and public issues, considering caste and regional factors.

articles.headline: Nitish behind security downgrade, vacation notices to Rabri: Lalu
articles.summary: RJD chief Lalu Prasad accused former Chief Minister Nitish Kumar of being responsible for the downgrade of his security cover and notices to Rabri Devi to vacate their Patna government-allotted residence. Mr. Prasad recently returned from Singapore. The bungalow has been allotted to Nand Kishor Ram, a Minister in CM Samrat Choudhary’s Cabinet.

articles.headline: Fire breaks out at Vijayawada children’s hospital; none hurt
articles.summary: A fire broke out at Rainbow Children’s Hospital in Vijayawada, Andhra Pradesh, on Wednesday, suspected due to an electric short circuit in an AC unit. No casualties were reported, and patients were shifted to a nearby hospital. Fire Department officials confirmed a thorough probe is underway.

articles.headline: Traveller booked for assault on Lufthansa crew member
articles.summary: An air passenger, B. Satish Kumar, was booked by Rajiv Gandhi International Airport police for allegedly assaulting a Lufthansa cabin crew member on flight LH-752 from Frankfurt to Hyderabad. The incident occurred after a snack trolley brushed against the passenger’s leg during in-flight service. A case was registered based on the crew’s complaint.

articles.headline: Stage set for Council election in Karnataka
articles.summary: On the eve of Legislative Council polls for seven seats in Karnataka, parties engaged in 'number churning'. Congress and BJP gathered legislators in resorts for mock voting. Congress is poised to win four, BJP two, with a tough fight for the seventh seat against JD(S). Congress currently has 37 members in the 75-member Upper House.

articles.headline: ATM of nationalised bank stolen in A.P; ₹18 lakh missing
articles.summary: Thieves stole an ATM of a nationalised bank in Anantapur district, Andhra Pradesh, early Wednesday. The burnt machine was later found in a field, with approximately ₹18 lakh missing. Police suspect three individuals and have formed special teams to apprehend the accused.

articles.headline: ‘Gaming company directors’ arrest illegal’
articles.summary: The High Court of Karnataka declared the arrest of Deepak Singh, Vikas Taneja, and Prithviraj Singh, directors of Gameskraft Technologies Pvt. Ltd., by the Directorate of Enforcement (ED) as contrary to law. The court ordered their release, noting the ED arrested them without summons and without new material, despite prior searches.

articles.headline: A.P.: ganja smuggling ring busted
articles.summary: Anakapalli police in Andhra Pradesh arrested five persons, including Shaikh Ajmal Khan with alleged links to Sri Lanka, and seized 213 kg of ganja and ₹95,000 in cash. Khan, with a criminal background in narcotics trafficking, was previously arrested by the Sri Lankan Narcotics Bureau for methamphetamine and hashish oil seizure.

articles.headline: Water security is central for a Viksit Bharat
articles.summary: Union Minister C. R. Patil highlights India's progress in water security through initiatives like Jal Jeevan Mission, Swachh Bharat Mission, and Namami Gange. These programs have increased tap water access to 81% of rural households, improved sanitation, created over 1.55 crore water conservation structures, and significantly reduced water-borne diseases and women's drudgery.

articles.headline: The existential crisis of the Trinamool
articles.summary: Shiv Sahay Singh argues that the Trinamool Congress faces an existential crisis due to a 'fan club' political model centered around Mamata Banerjee and Abhishek Banerjee, fostering opportunism. The defection of 60 MLAs and 20 MPs signifies a shift in power dynamics, stemming from the party's failure to address corruption, authoritarianism, and misrule, making it a victim of its own machinations.

articles.headline: Why A.P.’s incentive for having more children is misplaced
articles.summary: Sambavi Parthasarathy argues that Andhra Pradesh's one-time cash incentives (₹30,000 for third child, ₹40,000 for fourth) to boost population growth are inadequate. These amounts barely cover childbirth costs and fail to offset economic barriers like housing limitations and job insecurity, which are primary reasons for low fertility rates, as shown by UNFPA data. Such incentives cannot compensate for childcare costs.

articles.headline: Scheme for crocodile sanctuaries
articles.summary: In 1976, Dr. H.R. Bustard, an FAO (UNDP) expert, advised the Andhra Pradesh Government on crocodile conservation and farming. He identified gharial, maggar, and salt-water crocodiles facing extinction. Dr. Bustard found Corangi mangrove forest and Godavari river areas suitable for sanctuaries, and a scheme was to be prepared to declare them as such.

articles.headline: Oil pollution
articles.summary: In 1926, the International Oil Pollution Conferences in Washington passed a resolution recommending a 50-150 mile limit from maritime nations' coasts, within which oil discharge causing nuisance would be prohibited. Governments were urged to require ships flying their flags to refrain from discharging oil near other countries' shores, to prevent pollution.

articles.headline: The RBI and its growing fiscal role
articles.summary: Deepanshu Mohan and Ankur Singh analyze the RBI's record ₹2.87 lakh crore surplus transfer to the Union government, highlighting its increasing fiscal significance from reserve management and foreign assets. They question its impact on central bank independence, fiscal centralization, and the exclusion of such transfers from fiscal devolution to States, arguing it has become an increasingly important source of fiscal capacity.

articles.headline: What does the India-Russia logistics agreement allow?
articles.summary: Dinakar Peri explains the Reciprocal Exchange of Logistics Agreement (RELOS) between India and Russia, clarified as a standard military cooperation agreement similar to those with other nations. It allows reciprocal use of bases and ports for supplies, repair, and fuel during exercises, training, or HADR, not permanent troop stationing, and provides Arctic facility access. It simplifies administrative procedures.

articles.headline: The World Cup between the magic of football and the reality of power
articles.summary: Suresh Menon reviews three books (Soccer in Sun and Shadow, The Power and the Glory, World Cup Fever) that explore how the FIFA World Cup, despite its appeal, has been exploited by politics and power. He discusses instances from Mussolini's 1938 tournament to Qatar's 2022 hosting, highlighting 'sportswashing' and the increasing commercialization and geopolitical influences on the game, noting FIFA's willingness to embrace brutal regimes.

articles.headline: Manipur Governor discusses security situation with Shah
articles.summary: Manipur Governor Ajay Kumar Bhalla met Union Home Minister Amit Shah in Delhi to discuss the escalating security situation in the state, where ethnic violence between Kuki-Zo and Meitei communities has claimed over 250 lives since May 2023. The Governor updated the Minister on the challenges ahead of Assembly elections early next year. Two Deputy Chief Ministers representing Naga and Kuki communities took oath virtually.

articles.headline: U.S.-Iran deal could remove restrictions on Iran tankers
articles.summary: The U.S.-Iran MoU could lift restrictions on Iranian oil tankers, enabling their access to international banking, insurance, and credit. This follows U.S. punitive strikes on Iranian oil-carrying ships. While the U.S. asserts no tolls, Iran may levy service charges, raising concerns among international shipping companies about transit fees in the Strait of Hormuz. The deal could restore maritime traffic.

articles.headline: Indian, Thai officials talk on bilateral, defence ties
articles.summary: The 10th Thailand-India Defence Dialogue was held in Bangkok with both countries reviewing bilateral defence cooperation and exchanging views on regional and global security issues of mutual interest. Co-chaired by Admiral Nuttapol Diewvanich and Satyajit Mohanty, discussions covered the evolving security environment in the Indo-Pacific region and strengthening defence industry cooperation to promote stability.

articles.headline: Full body scanners on trial at four key airports
articles.summary: Full body scanners are on trial at Delhi, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, and Kochi airports, with Srinagar, Jammu, and Ayodhya next, to enhance threat detection and reduce physical frisking. BCAS issued SOPs for these advanced imaging technologies, which detect metallic and non-metallic items. CISF is evaluating their operational effectiveness for future policy decisions.

articles.headline: ‘Parasites’: SC denies bail to man accused of cyberfraud
articles.summary: The Supreme Court denied bail to a man accused of cyberfraud across multiple states, calling such offenders 'parasites' and advocating stern action. Chief Justice Surya Kant observed criminals commit offenses in one state and move elsewhere to avoid detection and arrest, stressing that society's interests are better protected when they remain in custody. He advised approaching the jurisdictional High Court.

articles.headline: Telegram was warned about misuse before blanket ban, says NTA chief
articles.summary: NTA Director-General Abhishek Singh stated Telegram was warned multiple times about the misuse of its features by 'paper leak mafias' to spread fake news and coordinate fraudulent activities before the temporary ban. He detailed how cheating syndicates exploited Telegram's group/channel synchronization to create convincing fake exam leaks by editing timestamps after the exam.

articles.headline: SC defers hearing of plea over suspension of NEET re-test to July
articles.summary: The Supreme Court deferred to July the hearing of a plea challenging the NTA’s decision to re-conduct NEET-UG for nearly 22 lakh candidates. The NEET-UG was cancelled nationwide on May 12 following leak allegations, and a re-examination is scheduled for June 21. Justice Narasimha's Bench, already hearing related cases, will hear the matter.

articles.headline: Ex-Trinamool Minister arrested in cheating case
articles.summary: Former Trinamool Congress Minister Udayan Guha was arrested from his residence in Kolkata for cheating and financial misappropriation, linked to allegations of collecting 'cut money' under the Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana since 2016. The complaint states he collected crores from residents to set up a children's unit at a government hospital in Cooch Behar district.

articles.headline: Rahul distracting students for political gain, says BJP leader
articles.summary: BJP spokesperson Sudhanshu Trivedi accused Congress leader Rahul Gandhi of distracting students for political gain with his Kota event, just three days before the NEET-UG re-test. Trivedi stated Gandhi was creating unnecessary stress and anxiety, pursuing a political agenda rather than focusing on student welfare or examination integrity, calling it a 'well-planned toolkit mindset'.

articles.headline: Rajya Sabha Chairman refers BJP MPs’ notice against Cong. chief to Privileges Committee
