articles.summary: Rajya Sabha Chairman C.P. Radhakrishnan referred a notice from six BJP MPs against Leader of Opposition Mallikarjun Kharge to the Committee of Privileges. The notice accuses Kharge of using 'derogatory, disparaging and highly disrespectful' remarks against Prime Minister Narendra Modi, lowering Parliament's dignity. Kharge clarified he never used 'terrorist' for the PM.

articles.headline: Bangladesh returns Assam man abducted from ‘no-man’s land’
articles.summary: The Border Guard Bangladesh (BGB) returned a 60-year-old Assam farmer, Ranjit Das, over 10 hours after he was allegedly abducted from the Indian side of the border in Cachar district. Das, working in the 'no-man's land' strip, was assaulted initially but treated fairly during his captivity, before being handed over to BSF. The BSF officer stated he was released after flag meetings.

articles.headline: Nicobarese tribal councils oppose draft election rules
articles.summary: Nicobarese tribal councils from three islands opposed the draft election rules for their self-governance system, warning against 'election rivalry, division, and conflict'. They argue their traditional, consensus-based systems align more with democratic values than new Election Commission-conducted polls which would reorganise villages into constituencies and prescribe five-yearly elections, interfering with age-old ways.

articles.headline: Tomato, onion, potato prices rise; farmers get low returns
articles.summary: Prices of tomatoes, onions, and potatoes have increased across most States, with tomatoes doubling in Delhi. Farmers' bodies claim they sell produce at very low prices, with potato farmers getting ₹2-3/kg, while consumers pay high retail prices. SKM warns of protests over fertilizer shortage, blaming corporate houses and big traders for gaining from the price rise.

articles.headline: Rural workers, activists call for repeal of new jobs Act; to stage protest from July 1
articles.summary: The NREGA Sangharsh Morcha and agricultural worker unions announced an indefinite protest from July 1, demanding the repeal of the new Viksit Bharat-Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) (VB-GRAM G) scheme, which replaces MGNREGA. They argue it reduces guaranteed work days to 42 and places an unprecedented 40% cost burden on states.

articles.headline: SC Bench says it will look into allegation of Registry misplacing case records
articles.summary: The Supreme Court took serious exception to an allegation that its Registry misplaced a case file related to an anticipatory bail plea. Chief Justice Surya Kant called it a 'very serious matter' and 'inefficiency' warranting an inquiry, asking the advocate to lodge a formal complaint with relevant details, stressing his concern over the Registry's functioning.

articles.headline: India, U.K. announce July 15 as trade deal implementation date
articles.summary: India and the U.K. announced July 15 as the implementation date for their Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA), overcoming last-minute differences over steel import duties. The deal, signed in July 2025, involves the U.K. removing tariffs on 99% of Indian product lines and implementing a Social Security Agreement. Both sides agreed to protect commercial interests.

articles.headline: Peace deal: cautious Indian shipowners seek govt. help
articles.summary: Indian shipowners remain cautious about the U.S.-Iran peace deal, anticipating 30-60 days for normalcy. They seek government help to evacuate 34 ships, including 16 fertilizer vessels, from the Persian Gulf due to war-risk insurance issues and high premiums. Naval escorts are being considered for safe transit, as the Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool has been a non-starter.

articles.headline: RBI allows banks to offer higher interest to NRIs/PIOs
articles.summary: The RBI temporarily withdrew interest rate ceilings on fresh FCNR(B) deposits (3-5 year tenors) and NRE deposits (three years and above) until September 30, 2026. This move, a review of its 2025 direction, aims to boost forex reserves by allowing banks to offer higher interest rates to NRIs/PIOs, aligning with domestic rupee term deposits.

articles.headline: India’s crude oil, LNG imports surge in May
articles.summary: India's crude oil imports grew 7.5% and LNG imports increased 16% in May compared to April, with the import bill surging 14.7% and over 22% respectively. Compared to May last year, crude imports surged 1.41% by volume but 81.6% by value, reflecting the impact of the West Asia crisis. Crude imports were 21.6 MMT in May.

articles.headline: BMW eyes sales of 20,000 units in India
articles.summary: German automaker BMW aims to sell over 20,000 units in India in 2026, driven by strong luxury vehicle demand. The company sold 18,000 vehicles last year, including 800 MINI cars, and reported a 17% year-on-year sales increase in Q1 2026. They plan to double MINI sales, noting the luxury car segment consistently outperforms the mass-market.

articles.headline: Oil prices tumble on likely U.S.-Iran deal
articles.summary: Oil prices fell to a three-and-a-half month low on Wednesday, with benchmark Brent crude futures declining 1.52% to $77.76 per barrel, due to optimism over a potential U.S.-Iran peace deal. Analysts caution a complete return to normalcy will take 8-9 months, and countries may rush to boost reserves. Lower crude prices are positive for inflation.

articles.headline: India to meet 3% compressed biogas blending goal for FY27
articles.summary: The Centre expects to meet its FY27 goal of blending 3% compressed biogas (CBG) with CNG and domestic piped natural gas, with current blending at nearly 2%. With 324 CBG/bio-CNG plants under construction and 1,261 more planned, this initiative aims to boost domestic energy security and reduce reliance on imported fuels. CBG sales were 0.66 mmscmd in April.

articles.headline: Rupee up for fourth session in row on softer oil price
articles.summary: The Indian Rupee appreciated for the fourth consecutive session, closing at ₹94.50 a dollar (up 10 paisa) on Wednesday. This gain was driven by softening crude prices due to eased U.S.-Iran tensions, outperforming Asian peers. Analysts indicate resistance at 95.10 for spot USD-INR, with benefits playing out over coming quarters.

articles.headline: Putin hosts ASEAN leaders as West mounts pressure on Ukraine war
articles.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin hosts a summit of 11 Southeast Asian nations in Kazan. Facing Western sanctions over the Ukraine conflict, Moscow is pivoting its economy towards Asia. Leaders will 'exchange views on global and regional problems' and discuss new aims in Russia-ASEAN ties, according to Moscow, from Wednesday.

articles.headline: EU lawmakers vote to approve ‘return hubs’ migration reform
articles.summary: European lawmakers approved tougher migration rules on Wednesday, granting broader detention powers and allowing 'return hubs' (deportation centers) outside the bloc. The 418 to 218 vote marks a significant hurdle overcome for the reform which aims to streamline migration management across the EU, despite concerns over potential human rights implications.

articles.headline: South Korea to ease civilian curbs along border with North Korea
articles.summary: South Korea's Defence Ministry announced new rules easing civilian access to the highly militarized border zone, allowing people to get several kilometers nearer North Korea. The Civilian Control Line will be shrunk to an average of six km starting 2027, broadening public access. Defence Minister Ahn Gyu-back made the announcement.

articles.headline: Israeli settlers torch two West Bank mosques, say Palestinian officials
articles.summary: Palestinian officials reported Israeli settlers set fire to mosques in two West Bank villages, with signs of arson and vandalism. Israel’s military confirmed finding two burned mosques and graffiti, stating forces searched for suspects who fled before their arrival. The incidents occurred in Jiljiliya, escalating regional tensions.

articles.headline: Elon Musk’s AI tool Grok was used in strikes against Iran: U.S.
articles.summary: A U.S. government legal brief revealed Elon Musk’s AI tool Grok was used in strikes against Iran. Pentagon AI chief Cameron Stanley testified that Grok, part of Project Maven, enabled U.S. forces to deploy over 2,000 munitions to 2,000 targets within 96 hours during Operation Epic Fury, praising its operational efficiency. The government terminated contracts with Anthropic earlier.

articles.headline: Messi hat-trick headlines Argentina’s romp past Algeria
articles.summary: Lionel Messi scored a dazzling hat-trick for Argentina, equaling Miroslav Klose’s all-time record of 16 goals at World Cups, as the defending champion swept Algeria aside 3-0. Messi, playing his 200th international match, became the first player to feature in six World Cup tournaments, sparking a frenzy among fans in Kansas City. Argentina seeks to be the first back-to-back winner since 1962.

articles.headline: Mbappe magic helps France pass Senegal test
articles.summary: France beat Senegal 3-1 in their FIFA World Cup Group I opener, with Kylian Mbappe scoring twice, including a late right-footer, and Bradley Barcola adding another. Despite the scoreline, Didier Deschamps’ side felt uncomfortable for much of the match against a spirited Senegal team at the MetLife Stadium. Senegal's early goals were disallowed.

articles.headline: Wissa’s strike helps a battling DR Congo hold Portugal
articles.summary: DR Congo held Portugal to a 1-1 draw in the World Cup. Joao Neves opened scoring for Portugal in the sixth minute, but Yoane Wissa equalized for Congo in first-half injury time, securing their first-ever World Cup point. Cristiano Ronaldo featured in a record-equalling sixth World Cup, playing in Houston.

articles.headline: Messi is a madman: Haaland in awe of the Argentine superstar
articles.summary: Norway’s Erling Haaland expressed awe for Lionel Messi, calling him a 'madman' after Messi's hat-trick against Algeria. Haaland shared a selfie on Snapchat with the caption, 'Messi is a madman' along with a crown emoji, acknowledging the Argentine legend's performance. This interaction highlights the mutual respect among top football players.

articles.headline: Haaland double powers Norway’s big win over Iraq
articles.summary: Erling Haaland scored his first World Cup goals with a double, leading Norway to a 4-1 victory over Iraq in Boston. This marked Norway's first game at a major tournament in 26 years, placing them atop Group I on goal difference, level with France. The Norwegians will face Senegal in their next game on June 22.

articles.headline: Norway fields three children of former World Cup stars
articles.summary: In a World Cup first, Norway fielded three sons of former World Cup players in the same match against Iraq: Erling (son of Alf-Inge Haaland), Alexander (son of Goran Sorloth), and Kristian (son of Erik Thorstvedt). Their fathers had played against Italy on June 23, 1994, making it a unique legacy moment in football history.

articles.headline: Women’s cricket taking off in a big way, says Maroof
articles.summary: Maroof Khan, a scout for the England women’s cricket team, notes the rapid increase in popularity of women's cricket, citing India's WPL and ODI World Cup triumph, and England's The Hundred. He believes the game will grow bigger, leading to brilliant young players from England, and notes increasing awareness around the game.

articles.headline: Manpreet scales a World Cup peak
articles.summary: Manpreet Singh became India’s most-capped hockey player with his 413th appearance during the FIH Pro League match against Germany, surpassing Dilip Tirkey. Hockey India announced a ₹10 lakh reward for the 33-year-old midfielder, as India defeated Germany 3-1. Goals for India came from Mandeep Singh, Shilanand Lakra, and Nilakanta Sharma.

articles.headline: Kiwis fight back on day 1
articles.summary: Tom Blundell scored New Zealand’s first fifty of the series, and Glenn Phillips posted an unbeaten 49, helping the tourists reach 291 for seven against England on day one of the second Test at the Oval on Wednesday. New Zealand played 77 overs in their first innings. England won the toss and elected to field.

articles.headline: India-A routs Afghanistan-A, all but confirms final berth
articles.summary: India-A secured a commanding 101-run win over Afghanistan-A in the tri-nation series, almost sealing a final spot. Half-centuries from Priyansh Arya (58), skipper Tilak Varma (59), and Kumar Kushagra (58), including a 104-run fourth-wicket stand, helped India-A post 319/9 before Nishant Sindhu's 4/31 bowled out Afghanistan-A for 218. The win lifted India-A to the top of the table.

articles.headline: Sooryavanshi, Halambage and Tilak face fines
articles.summary: India-A's Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Sri Lanka-A's Vishen Halambage face proposed fines of 50% of their match fees, while India-A captain Tilak faces a 30% sanction, following heated exchanges during a recent match. The match referee recommended disciplinary sanctions to the respective cricket boards, arising from sledging incidents, including taunts about IPL performances.

articles.headline: Scheffler seeks career Slam as Europeans eye Major treble
articles.summary: Top-ranked Scottie Scheffler chases a career Grand Slam at this week’s US Open, while European golfers aim for an unprecedented Major treble. Scheffler, targeting his first Major of the year, could become only the seventh player to complete the career Slam by winning on Sunday. Rory McIlroy warns of Shinnecock's challenging layout, demanding patience.

articles.headline: India looks to continue its winning run against Uruguay
articles.summary: A relaxed Indian women’s hockey team aims to remain undefeated against lower-ranked Uruguay in the FIH Nations Cup, having already sealed a semifinal spot. Despite scoring all five goals from penalty corners, chief coach Sjoerd Marijne expects strikers to convert more field efforts and emphasizes discipline. The team aims to win the tournament to return to the elite FIH Pro League.

articles.headline: Spinners Zampa and Davies set up Australia’s win
articles.summary: Slow bowlers Adam Zampa (3/18) and Joel Davies (3/17) combined for six wickets, leading Australia to a four-wicket victory over Bangladesh in the opening T20I series game at Chattogram. Australia chased Bangladesh's 131, reaching 133/6 in 18.2 overs, with Cooper Connolly top-scoring at 47. Zampa was named Player of the Match.

articles.headline: India beats Mongolia, makes it to the quarterfinals
articles.summary: India secured a 3-1 win over Mongolia in the World Team Snooker Championships at Guangdong, China, booking a place in the quarterfinals where they will face China 'B'. Pankaj Advani notably scored 70(51) against Sergelen Batdelger. Aditya Mehta also contributed to India's victory against Mongolia, allowing them to advance in the tournament.

articles.headline: Anmol, Rakshitha and Shriyanshi in pre-quarterfinals
articles.summary: Anmol Kharb, Rakshitha Sree Ramraj, and Shriyanshi Bainsla advanced to the pre-quarterfinals of the Macau Open Super 300 badminton tournament. Anmol brushed aside Indonesia’s Mutiara Ayu Puspidasari 21-13, 21-16 in just 42 minutes. Rounak Chouhan also progressed in men's singles after rallying from a game down.

articles.headline: Man City legend Silva joins Real on a two-year deal
articles.summary: Manchester City great Bernardo Silva has joined Real Madrid on a two-year contract, the Spanish club announced on Wednesday. Silva, a key figure for City, makes a high-profile move to the La Liga giants. This transfer marks a significant development in the football world, impacting both clubs' future strategies.

articles.headline: Deepti rediscovering her mojo augurs well for the Women in Blue
articles.summary: Deepti Sharma, India's off-spinning all-rounder, has rediscovered her form, vaulting back to the world's highest wicket-taker in T20Is with a match-winning five-wicket haul against Pakistan in the World Cup. Her crucial 12 not out and partnerships, alongside her consistent all-round contributions, make her a vital player for India, showing she thrives under pressure in ICC tournaments.

articles.headline: Dhaka’s new Padma barrage will reshape water power in the region
articles.summary: Bangladesh signed off on a new 2.1-km Padma barrage on the Ganga river, 180 km downstream of India's Farakka barrage, to abate seasonal water scarcity for 6.5 crore people. Estimated at ₹39,170 crore, it will store 2,900 million cu. m of water. The project raises environmental concerns and diplomatic tensions, as existing treaties like the 1996 Ganges Water Treaty are set to expire.

articles.headline: The universe’s expansion still accelerating: researchers
articles.summary: Researchers confirmed the universe's accelerated expansion, debunking a 2025 study. Analyzing Type Ia supernovae, they found no evidence for the 'age effect' that claimed acceleration had stopped. The findings reinforce the existence of dark energy, the cosmic force driving this expansion, contradicting prior challenges to fundamental cosmological understanding. This type of supernova helps in investigating the universe’s structure and expansion rate.

articles.headline: Karnataka CM puts over ₹100cr projects under CMO's watch
articles.summary: Karnataka CM D K Shivakumar is strengthening the project monitoring unit (PMU) in his office to oversee infrastructure projects valued at ₹100 crore and above, ahead of elections. This aims to accelerate key works and improve the government's performance record.

articles.headline: Mysuru hospital denies lapses after 8 ICU deaths in 24 hours
articles.summary: The Mysuru unit of Sri Jayadeva Institute of Cardiovascular Sciences denied negligence or staff shortage after 8 ICU patients died in 24 hours. The hospital director stated patients had severe pre-existing complications, dismissing claims of medical negligence.

articles.headline: US-Iran deal draft includes Hormuz transit, sanctions lift, $300bn fund
articles.summary: A US-Iran peace deal draft outlines diluting highly enriched uranium, ensuring toll-free Strait of Hormuz passage for 60 days, and lifting sanctions. It also features a $300 billion reconstruction plan and provisions for Lebanon's territorial integrity.

articles.headline: Rebel TMC MPs merge with NCPI, create national political talking point
articles.summary: Twenty rebel TMC LS MPs have turned the little-known Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI) into a national political talking point after merging with it, making it Bengal’s largest parliamentary bloc.

articles.headline: Madras HC stays church construction near Hindu temple in Coimbatore
articles.summary: Madras High Court stayed the construction of a proposed church near a Mariyamman temple in Coimbatore, citing communal sensitivity and the site being a public road, not patta land.

articles.headline: Six women killed by racing tractors crushing e-rickshaw in UP
articles.summary: Six women travelling to a pre-wedding ritual were killed and three others injured when two speeding tractors, allegedly racing, rammed and ran over their e-rickshaw in UP’s Budaun.

articles.headline: Karnataka considers single application for II PU exam, CET from next year
articles.summary: Karnataka is discussing integrating II PU exam and CET applications from next year to prevent students from making errors and submitting multiple forms. This move aims to streamline the process for science students and release results faster.

articles.headline: KMF clocks 1 crore litres milk daily, defies summer slump
articles.summary: Karnataka Milk Federation (KMF) recorded a daily milk collection of over 1 crore litres this summer, surpassing previous years. This surge is attributed to lucrative farmer pricing, government incentives, and early monsoon showers.

articles.headline: Karnataka police: Consent to record videos not license to share
articles.summary: Karnataka police mandated FIRs for non-consensual sharing of intimate photos or videos, clarifying criminal liability arises from unauthorized sharing regardless of initial consent. The directive aims to combat revenge pornography, sextortion, and online blackmail.

articles.headline: Kannada actor files complaint after AI post declares him dead
articles.summary: Senior Kannada actor Doddanna S filed a complaint after unidentified miscreants used AI tools to create and circulate a fake tribute post declaring him dead. The post caused emotional trauma and damaged his reputation.

articles.headline: Rottweiler bites 12-year-old boy in Bengaluru, owners booked
articles.summary: A 12-year-old boy was hospitalized after a neighbor's pet rottweiler mauled him in Mahadevapura, Bengaluru. Police registered a case against the dog owners, Shyam and Vijayalakshmi, who allegedly abused and threatened the victim's family.

articles.headline: Investors lose ₹6 crore in alleged SLV Mahila Mart scam
articles.summary: Tumakuru police reported that victims have lost at least ₹6 crore in the alleged SLV Mahila Mart scam. The women-led retail franchise collected investments from over 100 victims, promising a monthly return of ₹26,000 for every ₹1 lakh deposited.

articles.headline: BNCC completes 86% LED streetlight conversion in Bengaluru
articles.summary: BNCC has completed 86% of its project to replace conventional streetlights with LED lights across Bengaluru. So far, 68,907 LED streetlights have been installed out of 1.2 lakh conventional fixtures.

articles.headline: 16-year-old PU student dies after sports bike hits flyover wall
articles.summary: A 16-year-old II PU student succumbed to injuries after ramming his sports motorbike into the sidewall of a flyover on Old Madras Road in Dooravaninagar, Bengaluru, allegedly due to speed.

articles.headline: Woman's ears injured in gold earring snatching at Bengaluru bus stop
articles.summary: A 56-year-old woman sustained injuries to her ears when a masked miscreant ripped off her gold earrings at a bus stop on Hosur Road in Bengaluru. Police arrested Kanai Adhikari, 40, a construction laborer.

articles.headline: IIM-Bengaluru receives Tata Trusts grant for undergraduate school
articles.summary: Indian Institute of Management-Bengaluru (IIMB) announced a significant philanthropic commitment from Tata Trusts for its new undergraduate school. The school will offer BSc (hons.) in economics with data science and data science with economics.

articles.headline: HC upholds 1987 acquisition of 30 acres for trust housing project
articles.summary: Karnataka High Court reversed a 14-year-old single bench decision, upholding the 1987 acquisition of 30 acres in Nelamangala for Sri Prasanna Anjaneya Trust's housing project, which had already been developed with houses.

articles.headline: Internet use among Karnataka women jumps to 58%, says NFHS-6
articles.summary: Internet use among women aged 15-49 in Karnataka rose from 35% to 57.9% between 2019-21 and 2023-24, reflecting the state’s rapid digital transformation. Usage among men increased from 56.3% to 84.3%.

articles.headline: Karnataka to issue guidelines for medicos on respecting cadavers
articles.summary: Karnataka government will issue guidelines to medical students on appropriate behaviour and respect towards bodies donated for studies, following viral clips of students talking disrespectfully about cadavers.

articles.headline: HC directs Karnataka govt to formulate comprehensive homestay policy
articles.summary: Karnataka High Court suggested the tourism department formulate a comprehensive policy and regulatory framework for homestays, covering safety, compliance, and transparency, after a US national was sexually assaulted.

articles.headline: Cops consider lie detector test for suspects in Vennela murder case
articles.summary: Bengaluru police are contemplating a polygraph (lie detector) test on accused Praveen and Mohan in the alleged murder of six-year-old P Vennela to address contradictions in reconstructing the sequence of events.

articles.headline: National-level bodybuilder dies after workout in Davanagere
articles.summary: A 26-year-old national-level bodybuilder, Sushil Kumar, died allegedly due to cardiac arrest after a workout in Davanagere. He had won numerous awards and was preparing for an international competition.

articles.headline: Liquor seizure on Karnataka trains in May exceeds last 3 years' total
articles.summary: Authorities seized illegal liquor worth ₹83 lakh from Karnataka's rail stations in May alone, a figure surpassing the combined value of seizures over the previous three financial years, driven by two major busts.

articles.headline: Bengaluru police fine 230 for public smoking, tobacco violations
articles.summary: North Division police in Bengaluru launched a special enforcement drive, registering 24 cases under COTPA and imposing fines on 231 offenders for public smoking and tobacco-related violations.

articles.headline: Farmers protest Bidadi township, seek Rahul's help; BJP, JD(S) join
articles.summary: Opposition to the proposed Bidadi township project intensified as farmers launched a letter campaign to Rahul Gandhi, urging his intervention. BJP and JD(S) also stepped up pressure on the state government over land acquisition.

articles.headline: Karnataka votes for seven legislative council seats today
articles.summary: Biennial elections for seven Karnataka legislative council seats will be held today. While six seats are virtually decided for Congress and BJP, an intense contest for the seventh seat has triggered resort politics and voting drills.

articles.headline: Union Minister Kumaraswamy seeks relief for Karnataka mango farmers
articles.summary: Union minister H D Kumaraswamy urged cabinet colleague Shivraj Singh Chouhan to provide support for Karnataka’s distressed mango growers through the market intervention scheme, citing severe losses due to falling market prices.

articles.headline: Man arrested for wife's murder, staged as accident in Belagavi
articles.summary: A 33-year-old man was arrested by Gokak Rural police for allegedly murdering his wife by mixing poison in a cold drink and then staging the crime as a road accident to conceal his extramarital relationship.

articles.headline: Karnatak University suspends botany professor over harassment allegations
articles.summary: Karnatak University, Dharwad, suspended botany professor Devaraj Tangudorai following allegations of harassment by research scholars. An inquiry committee is investigating the complaints, with protests from students demanding action.

articles.headline: Karnataka, MP, TN top dole givers; subsidies triple since 2015-16
articles.summary: Karnataka, Madhya Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, and Punjab led in providing state subsidies, with 28 states spending nearly ₹4.4 lakh crore in 2024-25, a threefold jump since 2015-16. Energy subsidies accounted for 43% of payments.

articles.headline: SC directs HC to form bench without judge recusals for officer's case
articles.summary: The Supreme Court directed the Punjab and Haryana High Court to constitute a division bench to hear a case involving Indian Forest Service officer Sanjiv Chaturvedi, after over a dozen judges had recused themselves from the matter.

articles.headline: NPPA allows 20% price hike for SII's BCG, measles, MR vaccines
articles.summary: The National Pharmaceuticals Pricing Authority (NPPA) approved a 20% price hike for Serum Institute of India's BCG, measles, and measles-rubella vaccines. This move addresses rising costs and ensures sufficient availability of critical public health drugs.

articles.headline: Bhagwat asserts Maharana Pratap won Haldighati, accuses historians
articles.summary: RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat reignited debate over the Battle of Haldighati, asserting Maharana Pratap emerged victorious in 1576 and accusing historians of constructing a narrative that understated his resistance to Mughal rule.

articles.headline: Man arrested for attempting to kill teen daughter over transgender traits
articles.summary: Auraiya police arrested a man for attempting to kill his 14-year-old daughter. He allegedly beat her with a stick, threw her into a canal, and fled, reportedly targeting her over her transgender characteristics.

articles.headline: Sophisticated postholes with river sand found in Keeladi excavation
articles.summary: The Keeladi excavation team discovered postholes filled with river sand at a depth of two metres, a sophisticated technique designed to prevent termite infestation and decay in wooden posts, dating back to the 3rd century BC.

articles.headline: IAF flies 200 sorties to airlift NEET-UG papers to 18 zones
articles.summary: Indian Air Force carried out nearly 200 sorties over the past three-four days using Mi-17 helicopters and transport aircraft to ferry NEET-UG retest question papers to 18 designated zones nationwide, ensuring secure and timely dispatch.

articles.headline: UBT Sena denies 2 MPs signed rebel deal; Shinde Sena claims op complete
articles.summary: Shiv Sena (UBT) claims two of its six rebel MPs, Omraje Nimbalkar and Sanjay Dina Patil, have not signed the letter sent to the LS Speaker for recognition as a separate group, while Shinde Sena sources assert "Operation Tiger" is complete.

articles.headline: Sanjay Raut uses cuss words for defecting UBT Sena MPs
articles.summary: Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Sanjay Raut used cuss words such as ‘b...sdi’ and ‘g...u’ in a press conference to describe defecting MPs, sparking controversy over the normalization of profanity in political discourse.

articles.headline: Rote learning alien to traditional Indian education: NCERT Director
articles.summary: NCERT Director Prof Dinesh Prasad Saklani states that rote memorization was never part of traditional Indian education, which fostered critical thinking. NEP 2020 aims to revive this spirit by shifting towards understanding, creativity, and problem-solving, moving beyond marks.

articles.headline: Messi aide alleges TMC ex-minister interfered in Kolkata visit
articles.summary: Lionel Messi's chief adviser reportedly emailed Bidhannagar police commissioner, flagging former sports minister Aroop Biswas’s alleged interference and security protocol violation during Messi's Dec 2025 Kolkata visit, prompting his early exit from the field.

articles.headline: LS Speaker to hear Abhishek Banerjee on TMC split after rebel MPs merge
articles.summary: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla invited TMC general secretary Abhishek Banerjee for a meeting on June 19 to hear his case on the split in the Mamata Banerjee-led party after 20 rebel MPs merged with the Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI).

articles.headline: PM Modi at G7: Global South can't bear Iran war brunt alone
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi, addressing a G7 outreach session, stressed that vulnerable countries should not bear the burden of West Asia crises alone. He urged international financial institutions to support developing nations and proposed a global skills partnership.

articles.headline: Trump defends Iran MoU, threatens to 'go back to shooting' if unhappy
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump defended the US-Iran MoU, stating he is ready to ditch the deal and resume attacks on Iran if it doesn't meet his expectations, amid criticism of "staggering concessions" to Tehran.

articles.headline: UP’s tourism landscape transforms over 12 years, drives growth
articles.summary: Uttar Pradesh has emerged as a vibrant travel destination due to infrastructure development, enhanced connectivity, and revitalisation of cultural, spiritual, and heritage assets under PM Modi's vision, contributing to economic activity and employment.

articles.headline: German auto giants BMW, Mercedes-Benz face margin crash
articles.summary: German premium carmakers BMW and Mercedes-Benz saw core auto profit margins dwindle due to high costs, weak demand, and rising competition in China. BMW issued a profit warning, highlighting challenges for the broader auto sector.

articles.headline: BSE market cap scales $5 trillion again amid Dalal Street rally
articles.summary: BSE's market capitalisation surpassed $5 trillion again, reaching ₹475.1 lakh crore, fueled by a four-session rally on Dalal Street. A peace deal in West Asia lowered crude oil prices and lifted the rupee.

articles.headline: India-UK FTA, social security deal operational from July 15
articles.summary: The India-UK free trade agreement (FTA) and social security deal are set to be operationalized from July 15, following talks between PM Narendra Modi and his counterpart Keir Starmer. This boosts bilateral trade by eliminating tariffs on 99% of Indian exports.

articles.headline: Indian insurers want marine war cover pool to stay post Iran deal
articles.summary: Indian insurers prefer to retain the domestic marine war risk insurance pool (BMIP) as a buffer against future geopolitical shocks, despite easing hostilities between the US and Iran. They anticipate a gradual return to normalcy in global trade.

articles.headline: BMW India eyes No. 1 luxury spot with sixth year of double-digit growth
articles.summary: BMW India is targeting its sixth consecutive year of double-digit growth, with annual sales expected to cross 20,000 units for the first time. The momentum could propel BMW to the top spot in India’s luxury-car market in 2026.

articles.headline: US Federal Reserve holds rates, projects hike by end-2026
articles.summary: The US Federal Reserve held interest rates steady and raised its year-end inflation expectations, projecting a rate hike by the end of 2026. The world’s largest economy grapples with price jumps at a 3-year high.

articles.headline: ChatGPT's global market share drops below 50%; remains India's top AI
articles.summary: ChatGPT’s grip on the AI assistant market weakened, with its global market share slipping below 50% for the first time due to rising competition. It remains the leading AI assistant in India with a 45.6% share.

articles.headline: Cursor cofounder Aman Sanger gets $2.7bn boost with SpaceX deal
articles.summary: Aman Sanger, cofounder of AI coding startup Cursor, is set to see his fortune soar to $2.7 billion after SpaceX agreed to acquire the company in a $60 billion all-stock deal, a major transaction in the global AI landscape.

articles.headline: Milan prosecutors probe tram drivers' chat over female CCTV images
articles.summary: Milan prosecutors launched an investigation into a WhatsApp group where about a dozen tram drivers allegedly exchanged comments about images of female passengers captured by onboard security cameras, probing unauthorized IT access.

articles.headline: Sean Penn to direct movie on Jan 6 Capitol riot officer
articles.summary: Sean Penn will direct a movie about a police officer who was at the January 6 Capitol riot in 2021. Bradley Cooper is in talks to star in the as-yet untitled film, announced by Warner Bros.

articles.headline: Vietnam police rescue over 400 cats in cat meat crime bust
articles.summary: Police in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam, rescued more than 400 cats in a major bust of a cat meat crime ring. Over 40 rescued cats were reunited with owners, but several died due to harsh conditions.

articles.headline: Italian mother sues Meta, TikTok after daughter's suicide linked to algorithms
articles.summary: An Italian mother is suing Meta and TikTok after her 12-year-old daughter died by suicide, alleging social media algorithms fed her increasing self-harm content, which she described as a "devastating illness."

articles.headline: Hot mics at G7 capture leaders' banter on smoking, World Cup, Greenland
articles.summary: Hot mics at the G7 summit captured lighter, off-the-cuff conversations among world leaders, including Italian PM Giorgia Meloni’s smoking habits, discussions about the World Cup, and a cryptic mention of Greenland by US President Trump.

articles.headline: Woman, 16-month-old child recover from Ebola in eastern Congo
articles.summary: A 16-month-old baby and his mother recovered from Ebola in eastern Congo, a rare positive development amidst warnings that the outbreak could become the worst on record if it continues to spread.

articles.headline: UK report: Majority of grooming gang perpetrators are Pakistani Muslims
articles.summary: An independent UK report on grooming gangs reveals the majority of perpetrators were Pakistani Muslims, with at least 250,000 white British girls trafficked and abused. It criticizes successive governments for failing to confront ethnic patterns.

articles.headline: Louvre Director struggling to find funds for museum upgrades
articles.summary: The Louvre museum director announced struggles in securing funds to upgrade its ageing facilities, citing piling building-related emergencies and the infrastructure reaching the end of its life cycle, despite being the world's most visited museum.

articles.headline: Iran’s oil fleet ready to boost exports after US peace deal
articles.summary: At least three tankers carrying 5 million barrels of Iranian oil sailed through the US naval blockade this week, signaling Iran's readiness to resume exports following a US-Iran peace deal announcement scheduled for Friday.

articles.headline: Makerfield byelection could hold keys to UK Labour leadership
articles.summary: A high-stakes byelection in Makerfield, a Labour stronghold, could influence the UK Labour leadership as Andy Burnham stands for MP. Polls show a tight race, with potential implications for Keir Starmer's position.

articles.headline: Austria beats Jordan 3-1 for first World Cup win in 36 years
articles.summary: Austria won a World Cup game for the first time in 36 years with a 3-1 victory over debutant Jordan. An own goal and Marko Arnautovic’s penalty kick secured the win.

articles.headline: Uzbekistan makes World Cup debut against Colombia, led by Cannavaro
articles.summary: Coach Fabio Cannavaro leads Uzbekistan in their World Cup debut against Colombia, emphasizing confidence and fighting spirit. Central defender Abdukodir Khusanov is expected to play a key role in their defensive plan.

articles.headline: Foreign-born players surge to 25% in FIFA World Cup squads
articles.summary: The share of foreign-born players at the FIFA World Cup has risen sharply from less than 9% in 2006 to around 25% today. This trend, driven by relaxed eligibility rules, is reshaping international football.

articles.headline: Switzerland, Bosnia-Herzegovina seek advantage in pivotal World Cup clash
articles.summary: Switzerland and Bosnia and Herzegovina will look to turn opening draws into momentum when they meet in a pivotal World Cup Group B clash. All four teams in the group are tied on one point.

articles.headline: Canada remains mum on Alphonso Davies’ injury status
articles.summary: The status of Canada’s Alphonso Davies remains shrouded in secrecy ahead of their World Cup Group B clash with Qatar. Team officials are tight-lipped about his recovery from a hamstring injury.

articles.headline: Manpreet Singh eyes 2028 LA Olympics, remains most-capped hockey player
articles.summary: Indian hockey Olympian Manpreet Singh, now the most-capped Indian player with 413 games, aims for the 2028 LA Olympics. Driven by his daughters and meticulous fitness, he continues to perform at the highest level.

articles.headline: Teen amateur Zara Anand shares lead at Women’s Pro Golf Tour
articles.summary: Amateur Zara Anand carded a four-under 64 to share the first-round lead with Lavanya Jadon at the eighth leg of the Women’s Pro Golf Tour in Bengaluru. She bounced back strongly after a shaky start.

articles.headline: Studio Ghibli's 'Kiki’s Delivery Service' gets 10-episode live-action TV remake
articles.summary: BBC Studios, Wheel in Motion, and Kadokawa are adapting Studio Ghibli’s beloved 'Kiki’s Delivery Service' into a 10-episode live-action TV series. The series will trace a young witch's journey of independence, friendship, and her unique delivery service.

articles.headline: Kannada actor Sharmiela Mandre to marry Tamil producer Sudhan Sundaram
articles.summary: Kannada actor Sharmiela Mandre is set to get engaged to Tamil film producer Sudhan Sundaram at a private ceremony in Bengaluru next week. The couple confirms they have known each other for some time and are ready for the next step.

articles.headline: Bengaluru's 'walk a kilometer' campaign criticized over poor footpaths
articles.summary: Bengaluru residents criticized the city's "walk a kilometer" climate campaign by sharing photos of crumbling footpaths and unsafe streets, prompting the civic body to delete the post. The `2,000-crore road resurfacing program is welcomed, but pedestrian infrastructure remains a challenge.

articles.headline: Serena and Venus Williams reunite for Wimbledon doubles comeback
articles.summary: Tennis royalty Serena and Venus Williams are set to light up Wimbledon once again after receiving a doubles wild card for this year’s tournament. With a combined age of 90, the legendary sisters return to the All England Club.

articles.headline: Comedian Pranit More, Himanshu Jangra booked under IT Act
articles.summary: Gurugram Police booked comedian Pranit More and Himanshu Jangra under the IT Act and BNS following an NCW directive for their involvement in the ‘370 Biryani’ controversy. Both accused have been served notices to join the probe.

articles.headline: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce wedding rumors intensify for July in NYC
articles.summary: Speculation about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding intensified after reports of a massive stage being built and New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's cryptic mention of their wedding in July.

articles.headline: Actor R Madhavan plays golf with cricket legend Brett Lee
articles.summary: Actor R Madhavan played a game of golf with Australian cricket legend Brett Lee. Lee shared a pic, calling Madhavan one of the most respected actors and a genuinely top bloke.

articles.headline: Darshan, Vijayalakshmi move HC to stall 'Boss' film release
articles.summary: The Kannada film 'Boss' faces a new legal hurdle as actor Darshan and his wife, Vijayalakshmi, moved the High Court to stall its June 19 release. They object to the film’s depiction of events related to the Renukaswamy murder case.

articles.headline: The Chainsmokers announce three-city India tour in December
articles.summary: Grammy-winning EDM duo The Chainsmokers are set to return to India in December with a three-city tour. They will headline Sunburn Festival 2026 in Mumbai before performing in Delhi and Bengaluru.

articles.headline: NSE Files Draft Papers for India's Largest ₹30,000 Crore IPO
articles.summary: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) filed its DRHP with Sebi for an IPO estimated at ₹30,000 crore, potentially India's largest public issue, surpassing Hyundai. The offer is entirely for sale of up to 149 million shares, representing nearly 6% of paid-up capital, by institutional shareholders like SBI. This jumbo IPO, which could value NSE at ₹5 trillion, has faced regulatory setbacks for over a decade.

articles.headline: India-UK Trade, Social Security Pacts Effective July 15
articles.summary: India and the UK's Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) and a social security agreement will take effect on July 15, 2026, after ratification. This pact grants tariff-free access for 99% of India's exports, reduces duties on processed food, marine products, engineering goods, and auto components. Tariffs on Scotch whisky halve to 75%, and auto import duties cut to 10% over five years, deepening bilateral trade.

articles.headline: India-EU FTA to be Signed by Year-End 2026
articles.summary: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen announced that India and the EU will sign an ambitious Free Trade Agreement (FTA) by end-2026. Discussions with PM Modi at the G7 summit also covered boosting security and defence cooperation and accelerating an investment agreement, aiming to unlock huge opportunities for trade, investment, and diversifying supply chains.

articles.headline: CtrlS Datacenters Raises ₹7,000 Crore from CPP Investments
articles.summary: CtrlS Datacenters Ltd secured ₹7,000 crore from Canada Pension Plan Investment Board (CPP Investments) at a $4.8 billion valuation. This includes ₹4,000 crore for an 8.2% stake and ₹3,000 crore for a new JV for hyperscale data centers. This first major equity dilution aims to fund an expansion to 4 GW capacity by 2031, targeting 15-20% India market share amidst surging AI infrastructure demand.

articles.headline: Delhi HC Seeks Centre's Justification for Telegram Ban
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court refused immediate interim relief to Telegram against the Centre's temporary ban until June 22, citing NEET-UG re-examination paper leak concerns. The court issued notice to the Centre, seeking a response by Thursday, after Telegram called the blanket ban "grossly disproportionate," affecting 150 million users. The government cited "shocking material," while Telegram argued it had acted on complaints.

articles.headline: Modi, Trump Discuss Seafarer Safety, Trade at G7
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi met US President Donald Trump at the G7 Summit, discussing the safety of Indian seafarers in the Gulf after three were killed in a US strike near Oman. Modi stressed the "utmost importance" of keeping the Strait of Hormuz open and also discussed trade deals. Trump praised Modi, called him a 'killer' but 'angel', indicating a trade deal was close and suggested visiting India.

articles.headline: DS Group Boosts Hotel Investment to ₹1,500 Crore
articles.summary: Dharampal Satyapal (DS) Group is increasing its hospitality investment to ₹1,500 crore, accelerating expansion through acquisitions and new developments amidst a buoyant hotel market. This includes launching a 200-room W hotel in Delhi near IGI Airport, a partnership with Marriott International, set to open in H2 2027 with an estimated ₹400 crore investment.

articles.headline: Injured Kukis Shifted from Imphal Hospital Amid Protests
articles.summary: Three Kuki youths, injured in firing between Naga and Kuki villages in Kangpokpi, were shifted under heavy security from RIMS, Imphal, to Churachandpur Medical College. This followed protests and tear gas deployment at the hospital, as demonstrators tried to storm RIMS. Nurses decried security operations amid concerns over the deteriorating situation in Imphal. A suspected militant was killed in Churachandpur, but an MLA claimed he was a civilian.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi Criticizes Education System as 'Extortion Machine'
articles.summary: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi in Kota, Rajasthan, criticized India's education system as an "extortion machine" and "rejection system." He highlighted how families spend three times the Centre's education budget on five competitive exams (JEE, NEET, UPSC, SSC, RRB), leading to stress, financial ruin, depression, and suicides. Gandhi urged rebuilding the system to reduce costs and allow diverse dreams, avoiding direct attacks on the Centre over exam leaks.

articles.headline: Bengaluru Ranks 15th Globally in Startup Ecosystem Value
articles.summary: Bengaluru-Karnataka ranked 15th globally in the Global Startup Ecosystem Report 2026, with an ecosystem value of $153 billion. It drew $39 billion in venture funding (2021-25) and ranks 6th in Asia, with DeepTech as a key growth lever and 10/10 for cost efficiency. Both Bengaluru and Delhi slipped in global rankings but remain India's top hubs, highlighting new incubators and government support.

articles.headline: Tom Holland Confirms Marriage to Zendaya, Calls Her 'Best Friend'
articles.summary: Actor Tom Holland confirmed his marriage to long-time partner Zendaya, stating "I found my person" and calling her his "best friend." His family was present at the ceremony, a significant personal milestone for the celebrity couple. He emphasized the strength of their relationship amid fame's pressures.

articles.headline: India Dominates Netherlands in T20 World Cup Victory
articles.summary: India secured a dominant 95-run victory over the Netherlands in the Women's T20 World Cup, their biggest win by runs in tournament history. Openers Smriti Mandhana (74) and Shafali Verma (55) contributed, with Verma also taking 3/20 and Shree Charani 4/19. India posted their highest-ever T20 World Cup total of 209/5, securing their second consecutive win at Headingley.

articles.headline: Messi Equals Klose's World Cup Record; Mbappe Sets French Record
articles.summary: Lionel Messi scored a hat-trick against Algeria, reaching 16 World Cup goals, equaling Miroslav Klose's career record, and surpassing Kylian Mbappe. At 38, Messi became the first to play in six World Cups. Separately, Kylian Mbappé scored two goals in France's 3-1 win over Senegal, bringing his World Cup tally to 14, becoming France's highest-ever World Cup scorer and breaking France's all-time goal record (57th and 58th goals).

articles.headline: Haaland Scores Two Goals in Norway World Cup Debut
articles.summary: Norwegian star Erling Haaland scored two goals on his World Cup debut in a 4-1 match involving Norway and Iraq. This continues his prolific scoring, having previously scored on debut in the English Premier League and Champions League. Norway made its return to the World Cup after a 28-year absence.

articles.headline: Gill, Kishan Centuries Power India to ODI Series Win
articles.summary: Shubman Gill (154 off 110 balls) and Ishan Kishan (125 off 79 balls) scored centuries, forging a 224-run third-wicket partnership. They powered India to a massive 170-run victory over Afghanistan in the second ODI, securing an unassailable 2-0 series lead. India posted a mammoth 402/7, while Afghanistan was bowled out for 232, despite efforts from Rahmanullah Gurbaz and Rahmat Shah, with Darwish Rasooli retiring hurt.

articles.headline: Trump: Iran Peace Deal Expected 'Shortly', Warns of Bombing
articles.summary: President Donald Trump announced the Iran peace accord, expected to be signed "shortly," possibly Thursday or Friday, to end the West Asia war. Trump joked he'd take credit for success but blame JD Vance for failure. He warned he would resume bombing Iran if it didn't "behave" or violated the agreement, noting Iran agreed to dilute enriched uranium stocks and a copy was sent to Israel.

articles.headline: Trump Delays DNI Nomination to Push Voter ID Bill
articles.summary: President Donald Trump delayed Jay Clayton’s nomination as Director of National Intelligence to force Congress to act on a voter ID bill, linking surveillance program approval to its passage. He will keep Bill Pulte as acting director despite lawmaker opposition over Pulte's lack of intelligence experience. This move creates uncertainty for intelligence leadership and the expired Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act renewal.

articles.headline: Local Governance Reforms Proposed Amidst Election Preparations
articles.summary: New reforms for local self-governance are proposed, focusing on decentralization of powers and enhanced public participation. Details include funding mechanisms and administrative restructuring. This comes amidst ongoing preparations and political campaigning for local governance elections, with details emerging on candidate nominations, voter turnout predictions, and key local issues.

articles.headline: Economic Policy Debates: Outlook, Fiscal Adjustments, Growth
articles.summary: Expert analysis provides an outlook on future economic policies, including potential fiscal adjustments and monetary measures, forecasting short-term market reactions and long-term economic stability. Coverage of ongoing debates among policymakers and economists highlights discussions regarding the direction of economic policy, including debates on growth vs. equity and fiscal stimulus.

articles.headline: Public Health Campaigns Launched, Infrastructure Upgraded
articles.summary: New public health awareness campaigns are launched, targeting prevalent diseases and promoting healthy lifestyles through immunization drives and sanitation programs. Simultaneously, significant upgrades to public health infrastructure are underway, including new hospitals, diagnostic centers, and medical equipment procurement, to improve overall healthcare capacity.

articles.headline: Youth Sports Development Programs Launched Nationally
articles.summary: New sports development programs are launched, aimed at encouraging youth participation and identifying talent. Details on training facilities, coaching, and competitions are being established. These initiatives seek to boost youth engagement in sports, promoting physical activity and talent development at grassroots levels through both government and private sector programs.

articles.headline: CPP Investments to Invest ₹7,000 Crore in CtrlS
articles.summary: CtrlS Datacenters announced a strategic partnership with Canada Pension Plan Investment Board (CPP Investments), which will invest up to ₹7,000 crore. CPP Investments will acquire an 8.2% stake for ₹4,000 crore to accelerate CtrlS's growth in India's fast-growing digital infrastructure sector and meet the expanding digital economy's demand for data centers and infrastructure.

articles.headline: Ex-Kerala CM's Daughter Questioned by ED in Pay-Off Case
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) questioned T. Veena, daughter of former Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan, for around eight hours in Kochi. This was part of a money-laundering probe related to the Cochin Minerals and Rutile Ltd. (CMRL) pay-off case, focusing on ₹2.78 crore payments to her defunct Exalogic IT company and loans from Empower India Capital Investments Pvt. Ltd.

articles.headline: Prakash Raj Denies ₹200cr Allegations in Dharmasthala Case
articles.summary: Actor Prakash Raj clarified his limited involvement in the Dharmasthala investigation, confirming he only spoke to whistleblower Chinna C.N. alias Chinnaiah after the SIT probe began, reassuring him if he was honest. Raj denied allegations made against him in a writ petition, specifically refuting unsubstantiated claims involving ₹200 crore and stating he would pursue legal action. He urged against communalizing the issue.

articles.headline: Pentagon Drops 'Indo' From Pacific Command Name
articles.summary: The Pentagon is restoring the U.S. Indo-Pacific Command name to U.S. Pacific Command, reversing a 2018 decision. This change, which honors historical roots and doesn't alter its area of responsibility (western India to America’s Pacific coastline), was quietly made. It is laden with symbolism, triggering concerns about India's diminished strategic role in US Asia policy, eight years after being renamed to signal India's importance.

articles.headline: South Africa, Czechia Face Crucial World Cup Match
articles.summary: South Africa and Czechia face a crucial FIFA World Cup Group A match, with both teams under immense pressure after opening losses to Mexico and South Korea, respectively. Neither can afford another defeat to keep their World Cup hopes alive, requiring vastly improved performances. South Africa relies on defensive discipline and quick transitions, while Czechia prefers a physical approach with wide attacks for Patrik Schick.

articles.headline: Nataraj Breaks 50m Backstroke Record, Qualifies for Games
articles.summary: Olympian Srihari Nataraj broke his seven-year-old men’s 50m backstroke record, clocking 25.32 to retain his title at the Senior National Aquatics Championships in Ahmedabad. He also qualified for the Commonwealth and Asian Games. In a separate achievement, Saanvi Deshwal set a new women’s 200m medley record with 2:19.15, surpassing Hashika Ramachandra's three-year-old record.

articles.headline: UBT Sena Faces Split, Issues Whip to MPs
articles.summary: Six Shiv Sena (UBT) Lok Sabha MPs have written to Speaker Om Birla, claiming they constitute two-thirds of the party and seeking recognition as a separate group, signaling a potential split. In response, Shiv Sena (UBT) issued a whip directing all nine Lok Sabha MPs to attend a parliamentary party meeting and urged the Speaker not to entertain any claims from defecting MPs to form a new group or merge with another political party.

articles.headline: Lohia's 1946 Defiance Ignited Goa Liberation Movement
articles.summary: On June 18, 1946, socialist leader Ram Manohar Lohia openly defied Portuguese colonial laws in Margao, Goa, igniting a civil resistance movement. This pivotal moment, recalled by 94-year-old Rafael Viegas, led to Goa’s liberation 15 years later on December 19, 1961. This year marks the 80th anniversary of Lohia's defining act in Goa's freedom struggle.
